title: Domain Research port and RLM adapter
description:
## Goal / Problem

The AI Challenger (20c.4) generates better challenges when informed by domain-specific research — competitive landscape, industry patterns, common failure modes. This ticket adds a `DomainResearchPort` protocol and an RLM-based adapter that iteratively searches for domain knowledge before Round 2 begins.

Key constraint from spike Section 9: **RLM improves retrieval quality but does NOT solve hallucination.** Every researched fact must carry source attribution and requires user confirmation before entering the domain model. The Knowledge Trust Hierarchy applies: researched facts are Level 3 (AI-RESEARCHED) until user confirms them to Level 2 (USER-CONFIRMED).

## Background / Context

- Spike research: `docs/research/20260305_ai_assisted_ddd_session_design.md` — Section 9 (RLM Over RAG, Trust Hierarchy, Anti-Hallucination)
- RLM patterns: `docs/RLM.md` — Recursive Language Model REPL loop pattern
- **Pattern reference:** `src/application/ports/` — existing port protocol pattern
- **Pattern reference:** `src/infrastructure/external/anthropic_challenger_adapter.py` — LLM adapter with fallback

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Guided Discovery |
| Ubiquitous Language | DomainResearch, ResearchBriefing, ResearchFinding, TrustLevel, SourceAttribution, WebSearchPort |
| Layer | Domain (Research VOs) + Application (port protocols) + Infrastructure (RLM adapter, web search adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `TrustLevel` | Enum (IntEnum) | USER_STATED=1 / USER_CONFIRMED=2 / AI_RESEARCHED=3 / AI_INFERRED=4 (lower = higher trust, orderable via IntEnum) |
| `Confidence` | Enum | HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW |
| `SourceAttribution` | Value Object (frozen) | url, title, retrieved_date, confidence: Confidence |
| `ResearchFinding` | Value Object (frozen) | content, source: SourceAttribution, trust_level: TrustLevel, domain_area, outdated: bool |
| `ResearchBriefing` | Value Object (frozen) | findings: tuple[ResearchFinding], no_data_areas: tuple[str], summary |
| `WebSearchResult` | Value Object (frozen) | url, title, snippet, retrieved_date |

### Port Protocols

| Port | Method | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `DomainResearchPort` | `research(model: DomainModel, max_areas: int = 5) -> ResearchBriefing` | Takes full DomainModel (matches ChallengerPort pattern), returns structured briefing |
| `WebSearchPort` | `search(query: str, max_results: int = 10) -> tuple[WebSearchResult, ...]` | Pluggable web search backend. Separates search mechanism from RLM logic |

**Why two ports:** `DomainResearchPort` is the high-level interface consumed by ChallengeHandler. `WebSearchPort` is the low-level search mechanism consumed by the RLM adapter internally. This follows ISP — ChallengeHandler never sees web search details, and the search backend is swappable (DuckDuckGo, Google, MCP-based, mock).

### Adapters

| Adapter | Implements | Dependencies | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|-------------|---------|
| `RlmResearchAdapter` | `DomainResearchPort` | `LLMClient`, `WebSearchPort` | Iterative RLM search loop: search → synthesize → refine → search again. Uses LLMClient for synthesis, WebSearchPort for search |
| `NoOpResearchAdapter` | `DomainResearchPort` | None | Returns empty ResearchBriefing with all areas as NO_DATA. Used when no LLM or no network configured |
| `DuckDuckGoSearchAdapter` | `WebSearchPort` | `duckduckgo-search` | Free, no-API-key web search. MVP search backend |

**Degradation strategy (single RLM adapter with internal fallback):**
- LLM available + network available → full RLM loop (search → synthesize → refine)
- LLM unavailable + network available → raw search results without AI synthesis (findings carry LOW confidence)
- Network unavailable → caught by RLM adapter, returns empty briefing with all areas as NO_DATA
- Neither available → NoOpResearchAdapter used at composition level (no network/LLM configured)

### Sequence / Flow

```
ChallengeHandler    DomainResearchPort    RlmResearchAdapter    WebSearchPort    LLMClient
    |                     |                      |                    |              |
    |-- research(model) ->|                      |                    |              |
    |                     |-- research(model) -->|                    |              |
    |                     |                      |-- search(q1) ---->|              |
    |                     |                      |<-- results -------|              |
    |                     |                      |-- synthesize() ---|------------->|
    |                     |                      |<-- refined query --|-------------|
    |                     |                      |-- search(q2) ---->|              |
    |                     |                      |<-- results -------|              |
    |                     |                      |-- synthesize() ---|------------->|
    |                     |                      |<-- findings ------|-------------|
    |                     |<-- ResearchBriefing -|                    |              |
    |<-- ResearchBriefing |                      |                    |              |
    |                                                                               |
    | (presents briefing to user at start of Pomodoro 2A)                          |
```

### "I Don't Know" Protocol (spike Section 9.6)

The RLM adapter must explicitly fall back to Socratic questions when research is unreliable:

**Triggers:** Domain-specific pricing/rates, legal/regulatory requirements, business model specifics, technical constraints of integrated systems.

**Behavior:** Areas matching these triggers are added to `no_data_areas` even if search returns results, because the results are unlikely to apply to the user's specific domain. The ChallengeHandler converts `no_data_areas` into Socratic questions: "I couldn't find reliable information about [X]. Can you tell me about [X] in your context?"

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | DomainResearchPort defines high-level interface; WebSearchPort defines search mechanism; RlmResearchAdapter does RLM loop; VOs carry data |
| **O**pen/Closed | New search backends (Google, Bing, MCP) added as new WebSearchPort adapters without changing RLM logic |
| **L**iskov Substitution | Any adapter implementing DomainResearchPort works (RLM, NoOp, mock). Any WebSearchPort backend works (DuckDuckGo, mock) |
| **I**nterface Segregation | `DomainResearchPort`: high-level `research(model)`. `WebSearchPort`: low-level `search(query)`. ChallengeHandler never sees search details |
| **D**ependency Inversion | ChallengeHandler depends on DomainResearchPort protocol; RlmResearchAdapter depends on WebSearchPort protocol; infrastructure provides concrete adapters |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
# tests/domain/models/test_research.py
test_trust_level_enum_values: All 4 levels present
test_trust_level_ordering: USER_STATED < USER_CONFIRMED < AI_RESEARCHED < AI_INFERRED (IntEnum, lower value = higher trust)
test_confidence_enum_values: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW
test_source_attribution_immutable: Frozen VO
test_source_attribution_requires_url: Empty URL raises InvariantViolationError
test_source_attribution_requires_title: Empty title raises InvariantViolationError
test_research_finding_has_trust_level: Every finding carries trust level
test_research_finding_outdated_flag: outdated=True when source >2yr old
test_research_briefing_separates_data_from_no_data: Findings vs no_data_areas
test_research_briefing_immutable: Frozen VO
test_web_search_result_immutable: Frozen VO

# tests/application/ports/test_research_ports.py
test_domain_research_port_protocol: runtime_checkable Protocol
test_web_search_port_protocol: runtime_checkable Protocol

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_rlm_research_adapter.py
test_satisfies_domain_research_port: isinstance check
test_adapter_returns_research_briefing: Full round-trip with mocked WebSearchPort + LLMClient
test_adapter_attributes_sources: Every finding has source URL
test_adapter_marks_old_sources: Sources >2yr old → outdated=True
test_adapter_categorizes_confidence: Multiple sources → HIGH, single → MEDIUM
test_adapter_reports_no_data_areas: Areas with no results listed
test_adapter_handles_search_failure: WebSearchPort error → graceful no-data fallback
test_adapter_degrades_without_llm: LLMUnavailableError → raw results, LOW confidence
test_adapter_performs_iterative_search: At least 2 search calls to WebSearchPort
test_adapter_i_dont_know_protocol: Domain-specific triggers → no_data_areas even with results

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_noop_research_adapter.py
test_satisfies_domain_research_port: isinstance check
test_returns_empty_briefing: All areas as NO_DATA
test_no_findings: Empty findings tuple

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_duckduckgo_search_adapter.py
test_satisfies_web_search_port: isinstance check
test_returns_web_search_results: Mocked search → WebSearchResult VOs
test_handles_network_error: Exception → empty tuple

# tests/application/commands/test_challenge_handler_research.py
test_research_runs_before_challenges: Research called before generate_challenges
test_no_data_triggers_socratic_questions: Areas without data become user questions
test_handler_works_without_research: DomainResearchPort=None → challenges still generated (backward compat)
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/models/test_research.py` → should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/research.py` — TrustLevel (IntEnum), Confidence, SourceAttribution, ResearchFinding, ResearchBriefing, WebSearchResult VOs
2. Create `src/application/ports/domain_research_port.py` — DomainResearchPort protocol
3. Create `src/application/ports/web_search_port.py` — WebSearchPort protocol
4. Create `src/infrastructure/external/rlm_research_adapter.py` — RLM iterative search adapter
5. Create `src/infrastructure/external/noop_research_adapter.py` — NoOp fallback adapter
6. Create `src/infrastructure/external/duckduckgo_search_adapter.py` — Web search backend
7. Update `src/application/commands/challenge_handler.py` — Add optional `domain_research: DomainResearchPort | None = None` param; research runs before challenge generation when provided
8. Update existing ChallengeHandler tests (`tests/application/commands/test_challenge_handler.py`) — pass `domain_research=None` to preserve backward compatibility
9. Wire into composition: AppContext 15→17 fields (`domain_research`, `web_search`)

Run: `uv run pytest` → should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify all findings carry source attribution
- Ensure no-data fallback works cleanly
- Verify "I Don't Know" protocol triggers correctly
- Run quality gates

## Steps

1. Add domain VOs: TrustLevel (IntEnum), Confidence, SourceAttribution, ResearchFinding, ResearchBriefing, WebSearchResult
2. Add DomainResearchPort protocol in application/ports — `research(model: DomainModel, ...) -> ResearchBriefing`
3. Add WebSearchPort protocol in application/ports — `search(query, max_results) -> tuple[WebSearchResult, ...]`
4. Add RLM research adapter in infrastructure/external — iterative search loop using WebSearchPort + LLMClient
5. Add NoOp research adapter in infrastructure/external — empty briefing fallback
6. Add DuckDuckGo search adapter in infrastructure/external — MVP web search backend
7. Update ChallengeHandler — add optional DomainResearchPort dependency; research before challenges
8. Update existing ChallengeHandler tests — backward compat with `domain_research=None`
9. Wire into composition: AppContext adds `domain_research` and `web_search` fields (both optional)
10. Update `tests/infrastructure/test_composition.py` field count 15→17

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TrustLevel` IntEnum with 4 levels, orderable (USER_STATED=1 highest trust, AI_INFERRED=4 lowest)
- [ ] `Confidence` enum with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW
- [ ] `ResearchFinding` VO carries content + source attribution + trust level + outdated flag
- [ ] `ResearchBriefing` separates findings from NO_DATA areas
- [ ] `DomainResearchPort` protocol takes `model: DomainModel` (matches ChallengerPort pattern)
- [ ] `WebSearchPort` protocol defined with `search(query) -> tuple[WebSearchResult, ...]`
- [ ] `RlmResearchAdapter` performs iterative web search (at least 2 search rounds)
- [ ] `NoOpResearchAdapter` returns empty briefing (used when no network/LLM configured)
- [ ] `DuckDuckGoSearchAdapter` implements WebSearchPort (MVP search backend, no API key needed)
- [ ] Every finding has a source URL (no unsourced facts)
- [ ] Sources older than 2 years flagged as `outdated=True`
- [ ] NO_DATA areas explicitly listed in briefing
- [ ] "I Don't Know" protocol: domain-specific pricing, legal, operational triggers → NO_DATA even if search returns results
- [ ] Network failures handled gracefully (empty briefing, not crash)
- [ ] Rate limiting / timeout on web search (configurable, sensible defaults)
- [ ] RLM adapter uses LLMClient from 20c.8 for search result synthesis
- [ ] When LLM unavailable (`LLMUnavailableError`), degrades to raw search results without AI synthesis (LOW confidence)
- [ ] When network unavailable, returns empty briefing with all areas as NO_DATA
- [ ] ChallengeHandler backward compatible: `domain_research=None` → challenges still generated without research
- [ ] Existing ChallengeHandler tests (20c.4) updated and still pass
- [ ] Composition: AppContext fields 15→17 (`domain_research`, `web_search`)
- [ ] DDD.md glossary updated with new terms (TrustLevel, Confidence, SourceAttribution, ResearchFinding, ResearchBriefing, WebSearchResult, WebSearchPort)
- [ ] >= 80% test coverage for new code

**Follow-up (not in this ticket):** Artifact provenance propagation — DDD.md output should display TrustLevel + Confidence per fact (spike Section 9.5). Tracked separately.

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No internet | Network unavailable | Empty briefing with all areas as NO_DATA |
| Niche domain | "underwater basket weaving marketplace" | Most areas NO_DATA, falls back to Socratic questions |
| Well-known domain | "e-commerce" | Rich findings with HIGH confidence |
| All sources outdated | Only pre-2024 results | All findings have `outdated=True` |
| Contradicting sources | Source A says X, Source B says Y | Both presented, user decides |
| No LLM configured | NoOp LLMClient | Raw search results, LOW confidence, no synthesis |
| Domain pricing query | "What should the service fee be?" | "I Don't Know" → NO_DATA (even if search has results) |
| ChallengeHandler without research | `domain_research=None` | Challenges generated normally (backward compat) |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest --coverage --min-coverage=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-20c.4 (Challenger — ChallengeHandler is the integration point)
- Depends on: alty-20c.8 (LLM Port infrastructure — provides LLMClient for search synthesis)
- Architecture: ADR-013 — Deep mode features have local fallback; Principle #2 updated for Express/Deep tiers
- Risk: Web search quality varies by domain. Mitigation: always present findings as suggestions, never as facts.
- Risk: RLM adapter complexity. Mitigation: start with simple 2-round search, iterate later.
- Risk: `duckduckgo-search` library stability. Mitigation: behind WebSearchPort protocol, swappable to any backend.
- Follow-up: Artifact provenance propagation to DDD.md output (spike Section 9.5) — separate ticket.

title: Customer Simulator for Round 3 scenario testing
description:
## Goal / Problem

After Round 2 produces DDD.md v2 (deeper model with canvases, invariants, and resolved ambiguities), Round 3 validates the model by tracing concrete scenarios through it. The Customer Simulator generates scenarios from the domain model and narrates them step-by-step through bounded contexts, commands, events, and policies — flagging where the model breaks or is silent.

This follows the pomodoro structure from the spike: Pomodoro 3A (Scenario Generation → Happy Path → Failure/Edge Cases → Policy Gap Scan → Tally) and optional Pomodoro 3B (Cross-Context → Scale → Policy Conflicts → Final Tally).

## Background / Context

- Spike research: \`docs/research/20260305_ai_assisted_ddd_session_design.md\` — Section 1 (Pomodoros 3A/3B), Role 4 (Customer Simulator)
- Anti-hallucination: Same report Section 9 — SCENARIO-FROM-MODEL pattern: generate scenarios only from existing model entities, never invent new concepts
- **Pattern reference:** \`src/domain/services/\` — domain service pattern

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Guided Discovery |
| Ubiquitous Language | Scenario, ScenarioType, ScenarioTrace, TraceStep, ScenarioResult, ModelGap |
| Layer | Domain (Scenario VOs + SimulatorService) + Application (SimulatorHandler orchestration) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| \`ScenarioType\` | Enum | HAPPY_PATH / FAILURE_MODE / EDGE_CASE / CROSS_CONTEXT / SCALE |
| \`Scenario\` | Value Object (frozen) | type, narrative_text, context_names, derived_from (question/invariant ID) |
| \`TraceStep\` | Value Object (frozen) | context, command_or_event, description, model_covers (bool/None) |
| \`ScenarioTrace\` | Value Object (frozen) | scenario, steps: tuple[TraceStep], gaps_found: tuple[ModelGap] |
| \`ModelGap\` | Value Object (frozen) | description, gap_type (MISSING_EVENT/MISSING_POLICY/SILENT_ON_FAILURE/etc) |
| \`ScenarioResult\` | Enum | VALIDATED / MODEL_INCOMPLETE / OUT_OF_SCOPE |

### Sequence / Flow

\`\`\`
SimulatorHandler      SimulatorService       LLM Port         User
    |                      |                    |               |
    |-- generate_scenarios |                    |               |
    |   (from DDD.md v2) ->|                    |               |
    |                      |-- build scenario ->|               |
    |                      |   deck from model  |               |
    |<-- scenario deck ----|                    |               |
    |                                                           |
    | for each scenario:                                        |
    |-- trace_scenario --->|                    |               |
    |                      |-- narrate steps -->|               |
    |                      |   through contexts |               |
    |<-- ScenarioTrace ----|                    |               |
    |                                                           |
    |-- present trace + gaps -------------------------------->|
    |<-- user verdict: VALIDATED / BREAKS / OUT_OF_SCOPE -----|
    |                                                           |
    |-- apply fixes if BREAKS -------------------------------->|
\`\`\`

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | SimulatorService generates scenarios; SimulatorHandler manages user interaction |
| **O**pen/Closed | New ScenarioType variants added without changing trace logic |
| **L**iskov Substitution | LLM port can be backed by any provider |
| **I**nterface Segregation | \`SimulatorPort\`: \`generate_scenarios(model) -> list[Scenario]\`, \`trace_scenario(scenario, model) -> ScenarioTrace\` |
| **D**ependency Inversion | SimulatorService depends on SimulatorPort protocol |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

\`\`\`
# tests/domain/models/test_scenario.py
test_scenario_type_enum_values: All 5 types present
test_scenario_vo_immutable: Frozen
test_scenario_requires_narrative: Empty narrative raises ValueError
test_scenario_derived_from_model: derived_from references existing model element
test_trace_step_vo: context + command_or_event + covers
test_model_gap_vo: description + gap_type
test_scenario_result_enum: VALIDATED, MODEL_INCOMPLETE, OUT_OF_SCOPE

# tests/domain/services/test_simulator_service.py
test_generates_happy_path_per_core_context: 1 happy path per Core context
test_generates_failure_mode_per_core_workflow: 1 failure per Core workflow
test_generates_edge_cases_from_invariants: Edge cases probe invariant boundaries
test_scenarios_only_use_existing_entities: No invented concepts
test_trace_walks_through_contexts: Steps reference real context names
test_trace_flags_silent_gaps: Model silent on a step → ModelGap recorded
test_empty_model_returns_no_scenarios: Graceful on empty input
test_respects_complexity_budget: Only Core subdomains get scenarios

# tests/application/commands/test_simulator_handler.py
test_scenario_round_lifecycle: Generate → trace → present → verdict
test_validated_scenario_no_changes: User confirms → no model change
test_breaks_scenario_records_gap: User says breaks → gap recorded for fix
test_out_of_scope_deferred: User says out of scope → recorded as future
test_tally_counts_correct: Tested/gaps/deferred counts accurate
\`\`\`

Run: \`uv run pytest tests/domain/models/test_scenario.py tests/domain/services/test_simulator_service.py\` → should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create \`src/domain/models/scenario.py\` — ScenarioType, Scenario, TraceStep, ScenarioTrace, ModelGap, ScenarioResult
2. Create \`src/domain/services/simulator_service.py\` — scenario generation + trace logic
3. Create \`src/application/ports/simulator_port.py\` — SimulatorPort protocol
4. Create \`src/application/commands/simulator_handler.py\` — orchestrates scenario rounds
5. Wire into DiscoverySession deep mode: ROUND_2_COMPLETE → SIMULATING transition

Run: \`uv run pytest\` → should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify scenarios use only existing model entities (SCENARIO-FROM-MODEL)
- Verify concrete narration with fictional names (Alice, Bob)
- Run quality gates

## Steps

1. Add Scenario domain models (VOs + enums)
2. Add SimulatorService domain service — scenario generation + trace
3. Add SimulatorPort protocol
4. Add SimulatorHandler — user interaction orchestration
5. Wire into deep mode state machine
6. Add CLI for scenario presentation, trace display, and verdict collection

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SimulatorService generates scenarios from DDD.md v2 model data
- [ ] 3 scenario types in Pomodoro 3A: happy path, failure mode, edge case
- [ ] 2 additional types in Pomodoro 3B: cross-context, scale
- [ ] Scenarios only reference existing model entities (SCENARIO-FROM-MODEL pattern)
- [ ] Traces walk through bounded contexts step-by-step
- [ ] Gaps flagged where model is silent or breaks
- [ ] User provides verdict per scenario: VALIDATED / MODEL_INCOMPLETE / OUT_OF_SCOPE
- [ ] Tally summarizes tested/gaps/deferred counts
- [ ] Only Core subdomains get scenarios (complexity budget respected)
- [ ] `AnthropicSimulatorAdapter` implements SimulatorPort using LLMClient from 20c.8
- [ ] `RuleBasedSimulatorAdapter` implements SimulatorPort without LLM (template-based scenarios, local fallback)
- [ ] When LLM unavailable (`LLMUnavailableError`), automatically falls back to rule-based adapter
- [ ] DDD.md glossary updated with new terms (ScenarioType, Scenario, TraceStep, ScenarioTrace, ModelGap, ScenarioResult)
- [ ] >= 80% test coverage for new code

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No Core subdomains | All Generic | No scenarios generated, skip Round 3 |
| Single workflow | Only one domain story | 1 happy path + 1 failure scenario |
| No invariants | Aggregates without invariants | No edge case scenarios (nothing to probe) |
| All validated | User confirms every scenario | Tally: N tested, 0 gaps, 0 deferred |
| All break | Every scenario reveals a gap | Large gap list, suggest continuing to 3B |

## Quality Gates

\`\`\`bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest --coverage --min-coverage=80
\`\`\`

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-20c.4 (Challenger — produces DDD.md v2 input for simulator)
- Depends on: alty-20c.3 (Express/Deep mode — provides ROUND_2_COMPLETE state)
- Depends on: alty-20c.8 (LLM Port infrastructure — provides LLMClient for Anthropic adapter)
- Architecture: ADR-013 — LLM behind infrastructure-internal LLMClient Protocol, provider-swappable
- Risk: Scenario narration quality depends on LLM. Rule-based fallback uses template-based narration.

title: Iteration diff display and user approval flow
description:
## Goal / Problem

As the domain model evolves through Rounds 1-3, users need to see **what changed** between iterations. Without a diff view, users lose track of accumulated changes and can't make informed decisions at pomodoro breaks. This ticket implements the artifact diff display and the user approval flow at each break checkpoint.

The diff is shown at each 5-minute break and at session completion. It summarizes changes as: added invariants, disambiguated terms, new stories, adjusted boundaries, new canvases.

## Background / Context

- Spike research: `docs/research/20260305_ai_assisted_ddd_session_design.md` — Section 3 (Artifact Iteration Protocol), Section 9 (Artifact Provenance Tracking)
- Break design: Same report Section 1 — breaks show diff + convergence metric
- **Pattern reference:** `src/infrastructure/cli/main.py` — existing CLI output patterns
- **Snapshot source:** `ArtifactGenerationHandler._build_model()` builds a `DomainModel` from discovery answers. At each round transition (ROUND_1_COMPLETE, ROUND_2_COMPLETE, SIMULATING→COMPLETED), the orchestrator captures a DomainModel snapshot by calling this method with current session state.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Guided Discovery |
| Ubiquitous Language | ArtifactDiff, DiffEntry, DiffType, ConvergenceMetric, ArtifactVersion |
| Layer | Domain (Diff VOs + DiffService) + Application (BreakHandler orchestration) + Infrastructure (CLI diff rendering) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `DiffType` | Enum | ADDED / MODIFIED / REMOVED / DISAMBIGUATED |
| `DiffEntry` | Value Object (frozen) | type, section (e.g. "Ubiquitous Language"), description, provenance (TrustLevel) |
| `ArtifactDiff` | Value Object (frozen) | from_version, to_version, entries: tuple[DiffEntry], convergence: ConvergenceMetric |
| `ConvergenceMetric` | Value Object (frozen) | invariants_delta, terms_delta, stories_delta, canvases_delta |
| `ArtifactVersion` | Value Object (frozen) | version_number (1/2/3), model: DomainModel — the full domain model snapshot at that round transition |

**Snapshot design:** `ArtifactVersion` wraps a `DomainModel` object (from `src/domain/models/domain_model.py`) plus a version number. The `DomainModel` contains bounded contexts, domain stories, aggregates, ubiquitous language, and invariants — everything needed to compute diffs. Snapshots are captured at each round transition by calling `ArtifactGenerationHandler._build_model(event)` with a synthetic `DiscoveryCompleted` event built from the session's current state.

### Sequence / Flow

```
BreakHandler         ArtifactGenHandler    DiffService            CLI Renderer
    |                     |                     |                      |
    |-- _build_model() -->|                     |                      |
    |<-- DomainModel v2 --|                     |                      |
    |                                            |                      |
    |-- compute_diff(v1_model, v2_model) ------->|                      |
    |<-- ArtifactDiff ---------------------------|                      |
    |                                                                   |
    |-- render_diff() ------------------------------------------------>|
    |                                                                   |-- displays:
    |                                                                   |   + 4 invariants added
    |                                                                   |   ~ 2 terms disambiguated
    |                                                                   |   + 2 failure stories
    |                                                                   |   Convergence: stabilizing
    |                                                                   |
    |-- prompt: continue or stop? ------------------------------------ >|
    |<-- user decision -------------------------------------------------|
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | DiffService computes diffs; BreakHandler orchestrates snapshot + diff + prompt; CLI renders |
| **O**pen/Closed | New DiffType variants added without changing diff computation |
| **L**iskov Substitution | N/A — no inheritance |
| **I**nterface Segregation | DiffService has single method: `compute(before, after) -> ArtifactDiff` |
| **D**ependency Inversion | BreakHandler depends on DiffService interface |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
# tests/domain/models/test_diff.py
test_diff_type_enum_values: ADDED, MODIFIED, REMOVED, DISAMBIGUATED
test_diff_entry_immutable: Frozen VO
test_diff_entry_has_provenance: Every entry carries TrustLevel
test_artifact_diff_contains_entries: entries tuple populated
test_convergence_metric_deltas: All 4 delta fields present
test_artifact_version_wraps_domain_model: version_number + DomainModel reference

# tests/domain/services/test_diff_service.py
test_computes_added_invariants: New invariant in v2 → ADDED entry
test_computes_disambiguated_terms: Term split in v2 → DISAMBIGUATED entry
test_computes_added_stories: New failure story → ADDED entry
test_computes_modified_boundaries: Context boundary changed → MODIFIED entry
test_computes_added_canvases: New canvas in v2 → ADDED entry
test_computes_removed_items: Item in v1 not in v2 → REMOVED entry
test_convergence_metric_accurate: Counts match actual diff entries
test_identical_snapshots_empty_diff: Same v1 and v2 → empty diff
test_provenance_preserved_in_diff: Diff entries carry source trust level

# tests/application/commands/test_break_handler.py
test_break_captures_snapshot: BreakHandler stores ArtifactVersion at transition
test_break_computes_diff: BreakHandler calls DiffService.compute() with two snapshots
test_break_returns_diff_for_rendering: BreakHandler returns ArtifactDiff to caller

# tests/infrastructure/cli/test_diff_rendering.py
test_diff_displayed_with_symbols: + for added, ~ for modified, - for removed
test_convergence_shown_at_break: Metric displayed with trend
test_continue_or_stop_prompt: User prompted at break
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/models/test_diff.py tests/domain/services/test_diff_service.py` → should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/artifact_diff.py` — DiffType, DiffEntry, ArtifactDiff, ConvergenceMetric, ArtifactVersion VOs
2. Create `src/domain/services/diff_service.py` — compares two DomainModel snapshots field-by-field (bounded contexts, stories, aggregates, ubiquitous language, canvases)
3. Create `src/application/commands/break_handler.py` — orchestrates: capture snapshot → compute diff → return for rendering
4. Add break rendering to CLI — diff display + convergence metric + continue/stop prompt
5. Wire into deep mode: breaks after Pomodoro 2A, 2B, 3A show diffs

Run: `uv run pytest` → should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify diff symbols render cleanly in terminal
- Ensure convergence metric is readable
- Run quality gates

## Steps

1. Add Diff domain models (VOs + enums) — `src/domain/models/artifact_diff.py`
2. Add DiffService domain service — compares two DomainModel objects by iterating bounded contexts, stories, aggregates, invariants, ubiquitous language terms, and canvases
3. Add BreakHandler application command — captures ArtifactVersion snapshots at round transitions by calling `ArtifactGenerationHandler._build_model()`, stores previous version, calls DiffService.compute()
4. Add CLI diff renderer — symbols, convergence, prompt (`src/infrastructure/cli/`)
5. Wire into deep mode break handlers — BreakHandler invoked at ROUND_1_COMPLETE, ROUND_2_COMPLETE, and SIMULATING→COMPLETED transitions
6. Update `composition.py` if BreakHandler needs AppContext wiring

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DiffService computes diff between two DomainModel snapshots
- [ ] 4 diff types supported: ADDED, MODIFIED, REMOVED, DISAMBIGUATED
- [ ] Every diff entry carries provenance (TrustLevel from source)
- [ ] ConvergenceMetric tracks invariants/terms/stories/canvases deltas
- [ ] ArtifactVersion wraps DomainModel + version_number (not opaque blob)
- [ ] BreakHandler captures DomainModel snapshot at each round transition via ArtifactGenerationHandler._build_model()
- [ ] CLI displays diff with `+` / `~` / `-` symbols at breaks
- [ ] Convergence trend shown (active refinement / stabilizing / converged)
- [ ] User prompted to continue or stop at each break
- [ ] Identical snapshots produce empty diff (no noise)
- [ ] Diff display truncates at reasonable length for large changes (grouped by section with summary count)
- [ ] Text-based diff output works standalone (terminal-first; VS Code extension will add visual diff rendering separately via `alty-vscode-extension`)
- [ ] DDD.md glossary updated with new terms (ArtifactDiff, DiffEntry, DiffType, ConvergenceMetric, ArtifactVersion)
- [ ] >= 80% test coverage for new code

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Identical snapshots | v1 == v2 | Empty diff, convergence delta = 0, suggest stopping |
| Massive changes | 20+ entries | Grouped by section, summary count at top |
| Only removals | Items removed in v2 | Show `-` entries, warn user |
| First diff (v1 to v2) | No prior diff | Show full diff, no trend comparison |
| Provenance mixed | Some USER_STATED, some AI_INFERRED | Entries color-coded by trust level |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest --coverage --min-coverage=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-20c.3 (Express/Deep mode — provides break states)
- Depends on: alty-20c.4 (Challenger — produces v1→v2 changes to diff)
- Enhanced by: alty-20c.5 (Research — provenance tracking in diff entries)
- **Snapshot dependency:** Requires access to `ArtifactGenerationHandler._build_model()` — may need to extract as a public method or create a dedicated snapshot service

title: LLM Port infrastructure: shared client, Anthropic adapter, local fallback
description:
## Goal / Problem

Deep mode features (Challenger, Simulator, Domain Research) need LLM capabilities, but the architecture must remain **provider-agnostic** and **offline-capable**. We need a shared LLM client abstraction in the infrastructure layer that adapters can use internally, with the `anthropic` SDK as the first implementation and rule-based fallbacks for offline use.

This is a **foundational ticket** — 20c.4 (Challenger), 20c.5 (Research), and 20c.6 (Simulator) all depend on this infrastructure.

## Background / Context

- Architecture: ADR-013 in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` section 7.8 — LLM Port Architecture
- Spike: `docs/research/20260305_ai_assisted_ddd_session_design.md` Section 9
- SDK evaluation: `docs/research/20260305_claude_agent_sdk_evaluation.md`
- Decision: Use `anthropic` SDK (direct API) not `claude-agent-sdk` (Agent SDK). Direct API is lighter (~50ms vs ~1-3s), more stable (v1.x vs v0.1.x alpha), and sufficient for structured output calls.
- Provider config via `.alty/config.toml` `[llm]` section

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Guided Discovery (Deep mode infrastructure) |
| Ubiquitous Language | LLMClient, LLMProvider, StructuredOutput, LocalFallback |
| Layer | Infrastructure only — this is NOT a domain or application concern. Domain-specific ports (ChallengerPort, SimulatorPort) are defined in their own tickets. This ticket provides the shared infrastructure client. |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Location | Purpose |
|-------|------|----------|---------|
| `LLMClient` | Protocol | `infrastructure/external/llm_client.py` | Infrastructure-internal abstraction for LLM providers |
| `LLMProvider` | Enum | `infrastructure/external/llm_client.py` | ANTHROPIC / OLLAMA / VERTEXAI / NONE |
| `LLMConfig` | Value Object (frozen) | `infrastructure/external/llm_client.py` | provider, model, fallback strategy |
| `LLMResponse` | Value Object (frozen) | `infrastructure/external/llm_client.py` | content, model_used, usage stats |

### LLMClient Protocol

```python
class LLMClient(Protocol):
    async def structured_output(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system: str,
        output_schema: dict[str, Any],
    ) -> dict[str, Any]: ...

    async def text_completion(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system: str,
        max_tokens: int = 4096,
    ) -> str: ...
```

### Sequence / Flow

```
Composition Root        LLMConfig       LLMClientFactory      Adapter
    |                      |                  |                  |
    |-- read config ------>|                  |                  |
    |-- create_client(config) --------------->|                  |
    |                                         |-- if anthropic ->|
    |                                         |   AnthropicClient|
    |                                         |-- if ollama ---->|
    |                                         |   OllamaClient   |
    |                                         |-- if none ------>|
    |                                         |   NoOpClient     |
    |<-- LLMClient instance ------------------|                  |
    |                                                            |
    |-- inject into ChallengerAdapter, SimulatorAdapter, etc.    |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | `LLMClient` does one thing: send prompt, get response. Each provider adapter wraps one SDK. |
| **O**pen/Closed | New providers added by implementing LLMClient Protocol — no changes to existing adapters |
| **L**iskov Substitution | All LLMClient implementations honor same Protocol. AnthropicClient, OllamaClient, NoOpClient all interchangeable. |
| **I**nterface Segregation | LLMClient has only 2 methods (structured_output, text_completion). Domain-specific ports have their own focused interfaces. |
| **D**ependency Inversion | ChallengerAdapter depends on LLMClient Protocol, not anthropic SDK directly |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
# tests/infrastructure/external/test_llm_client.py
test_llm_provider_enum_values: ANTHROPIC, OLLAMA, VERTEXAI, NONE
test_llm_config_immutable: Frozen dataclass
test_llm_config_defaults: provider=NONE, fallback=rule_based

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_anthropic_client.py
test_structured_output_returns_parsed_json: Mock httpx, verify schema enforcement
test_text_completion_returns_string: Mock httpx, verify response
test_handles_api_error_gracefully: APIError → raises LLMUnavailableError
test_handles_rate_limit: RateLimitError → raises LLMUnavailableError
test_handles_network_error: ConnectionError → raises LLMUnavailableError
test_timeout_configurable: Custom timeout honored

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_noop_client.py
test_noop_structured_output_raises: NoOpClient raises LLMUnavailableError
test_noop_text_completion_raises: NoOpClient raises LLMUnavailableError

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_llm_client_factory.py
test_factory_creates_anthropic_client: provider=ANTHROPIC → AnthropicLLMClient
test_factory_creates_noop_for_none: provider=NONE → NoOpLLMClient
test_factory_creates_noop_when_no_api_key: provider=ANTHROPIC but no key → NoOpLLMClient with warning
test_factory_reads_config_from_toml: Reads .alty/config.toml [llm] section
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/infrastructure/external/test_llm_client*.py` → should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/infrastructure/external/llm_client.py` — LLMClient Protocol, LLMProvider enum, LLMConfig VO, LLMResponse VO, LLMUnavailableError
2. Create `src/infrastructure/external/anthropic_llm_client.py` — Anthropic SDK adapter
3. Create `src/infrastructure/external/noop_llm_client.py` — NoOp client (raises LLMUnavailableError)
4. Create `src/infrastructure/external/llm_client_factory.py` — Factory that reads config and creates appropriate client
5. Update `src/infrastructure/composition.py` — Wire LLMClient into composition root

Run: `uv run pytest` → should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify all error paths are covered
- Ensure no anthropic SDK imports leak outside infrastructure layer
- Run quality gates

## Steps

1. Add LLMClient Protocol, LLMProvider enum, config VOs, LLMUnavailableError
2. Implement AnthropicLLMClient using `anthropic` SDK with structured output support
3. Implement NoOpLLMClient for offline/no-provider mode
4. Add LLMClientFactory with config-driven provider selection
5. Wire into composition root
6. Add `anthropic` as optional dependency in pyproject.toml (`[project.optional-dependencies] llm = ["anthropic>=0.40"]`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `LLMClient` Protocol with `structured_output()` and `text_completion()` methods
- [ ] `LLMProvider` enum: ANTHROPIC, OLLAMA, VERTEXAI, NONE
- [ ] `AnthropicLLMClient` implements LLMClient using `anthropic` SDK
- [ ] `NoOpLLMClient` implements LLMClient, raises `LLMUnavailableError` on all calls
- [ ] `LLMClientFactory` creates client from `.alty/config.toml` `[llm]` section
- [ ] Missing API key gracefully falls back to NoOpClient (not crash)
- [ ] API errors (rate limit, network, timeout) raise `LLMUnavailableError`
- [ ] `anthropic` is an optional dependency — alty works without it installed
- [ ] No anthropic SDK imports outside `infrastructure/external/`
- [ ] DDD.md glossary updated with new terms (LLMClient, LLMProvider, LLMUnavailableError)
- [ ] >= 80% test coverage for new code

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No API key set | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY missing | NoOpClient created, warning logged |
| anthropic not installed | `import anthropic` fails | NoOpClient created, warning logged |
| API rate limited | 429 response | LLMUnavailableError with retry hint |
| Network timeout | Connection timeout | LLMUnavailableError |
| Invalid schema response | LLM returns non-conforming JSON | LLMUnavailableError with details |
| Config missing [llm] section | No LLM config in .alty/config.toml | Defaults to provider=NONE |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest --coverage --min-coverage=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-20c.3 (Express/Deep mode — determines when LLM is needed)
- Risk: `anthropic` SDK version churn. Mitigation: pin `>=0.40,<1.0`, wrap all SDK calls in adapter.
- Risk: Structured output schema validation. Mitigation: Pydantic model validation in adapter, not domain.

title: Epic: Phase 7 — Ticket & Doc Health
description:
## Goal / Problem

alty's core pipeline (Phases 1–6) is complete: guided discovery → domain model → fitness functions → ticket pipeline → tool configs → rescue mode. But the generated projects have no living documentation system. Docs go stale, tickets drift from reality, and there's no per-project knowledge base. Phase 7 closes the loop by making alty-generated projects self-maintaining.

**The problem in one sentence:** alty generates excellent blueprints at t=0, but provides nothing to keep them accurate at t+N.

## Prior Art

- **Ripple review spike:** `docs/research/20260223_ripple_review_design.md` (k7m.12) — complete design for ripple lifecycle, two-tier generation, `alty ticket-health`, after-close protocol generation. **Read this before any child ticket.**
- **Existing domain scaffolding:** Ports (`DocHealthPort`, `KnowledgeLookupPort`, `TicketHealthPort`) and domain models (`TicketHealthReport`, `DocHealthReport`, `ticket_freshness.py`) already exist as stubs. This is NOT greenfield — the skeleton is wired.
- **`bin/bd-ripple`:** Already implements non-empty context enforcement, sibling/dependent/related traversal, deduplication. Domain migration is about bringing this logic under test, not rewriting from scratch.

## DDD Alignment

| Bounded Context | Classification | Impact |
|----------------|---------------|--------|
| Ticket Freshness | **Core** | Domain integration of ripple review lifecycle (currently a bash script outside the domain). Flagging, context diff capture, review flow, flag clearing — all through domain services. `alty ticket-health` CLI command. Two-tier ticket generation rules (near-term full AC vs far-term stubs). |
| Knowledge Base | **Supporting** | `.alty/knowledge/` directory with RLM-addressable docs for DDD patterns, tool conventions, coding standards. `alty kb <topic>` CLI command. |
| Bootstrap | **Supporting** (existing) | Extended to create `.alty/` directory structure during `alty init`. After-close protocol generated into bootstrapped project's CLAUDE.md. |
| Rescue | **Supporting** (existing) | Extended to detect missing `.alty/` in gap analysis. |

Verify with `/architecture-docs` commands:

| Check | Command |
|-------|---------|
| Domain model | `/architecture-docs domain` |
| Architecture | `/architecture-docs components` |

## Success Metrics

- `alty ticket-health` reports flagged tickets with context diffs, freshness percentage per epic (90–100% healthy, 70–89% acceptable, <70% action needed), oldest unreviewed ticket
- `alty doc-health` checks freshness, broken references, missing metadata for project docs
- `alty doc-review` marks docs as reviewed and updates `last_reviewed` dates
- `alty kb <topic>` returns RLM-addressable content from `.alty/knowledge/`
- `alty init` creates `.alty/` directory with config, knowledge seed, and maintenance registry
- `alty init --existing` detects missing `.alty/` as a gap
- Ripple review lifecycle is domain-driven (tested, not a bash script shelling out to `bd`)
- Two-tier generation: near-term tickets get full AC, far-term get stubs with promotion on unblock

## Scope

**In scope**

- `.alty/` project directory creation during `alty init` and `alty init --existing`
- `.alty/config.toml` — per-project alty settings
- `.alty/knowledge/` — RLM-addressable knowledge base (DDD patterns, tool conventions, coding standards)
- `.alty/maintenance/doc-registry.toml` — doc tracking, owners, review cadence
- `alty kb <topic>` — knowledge base lookup command (implements existing `KnowledgeLookupPort`)
- `alty doc-health` — freshness check, broken references, missing metadata (implements existing `DocHealthPort`)
- `alty doc-review` — mark docs as reviewed, update timestamps
- `alty ticket-health` — freshness report with per-epic rollup, flagged list, oldest unreviewed (implements existing `TicketHealthPort`)
- Domain model for Ticket Freshness context — bring `bin/bd-ripple` logic under domain services and tests (RippleReview aggregate, TicketFlagged/FlagCleared events)
- Two-tier ticket generation rules: depth-based classification (≤2 hops = near-term full, >2 = far-term stub), subdomain override (Core always full)
- After-close protocol generation into bootstrapped project's CLAUDE.md (spike §6.2–6.3)

**Out of scope**

- MCP server (Phase 5 — separate epic)
- Agent persona generation (Phase 4 gap — separate ticket)
- Knowledge base drift detection (P1 — future epic after base KB exists)
- Tool knowledge versioning beyond current version (P1)
- Additional StackProfiles (Go, Rust, TypeScript — separate epic)
- Auto-updating knowledge from external sources (P2)

## Phases / Milestones

1. **Spike & Design** — Research `.alty/` directory structure, RLM addressing scheme, doc-registry format. Validate spike design against current codebase.
2. **`.alty/` Foundation** — Create `.alty/` directory during init, seed knowledge content, integrate into gap analysis. After-close protocol in generated CLAUDE.md.
3. **Knowledge Base** — `alty kb <topic>` with RLM addressing. Implement `KnowledgeLookupPort` adapter.
4. **Doc Maintenance** — `alty doc-health` and `alty doc-review` with doc-registry tracking. Implement `DocHealthPort` adapter.
5. **Ticket Freshness Domain** — Bring `bin/bd-ripple` logic into domain services under test. `alty ticket-health` command. Implement `TicketHealthPort` adapter. Two-tier generation rules.

## Child Tasks (planned)

Tickets will be created after epic grooming. Expected breakdown:

- Spike: `.alty/` directory structure and RLM addressing scheme
- Spike: Validate ripple review spike design against current codebase
- Task: `.alty/` directory creation in Bootstrap and Rescue flows
- Task: After-close protocol generation into bootstrapped CLAUDE.md
- Task: Knowledge base seed content (DDD patterns, tool conventions)
- Task: `alty kb` command — implement `KnowledgeLookupPort`
- Task: `alty doc-health` command — implement `DocHealthPort`
- Task: `alty doc-review` command
- Task: Ticket Freshness domain model — bring `bd-ripple` logic under domain services
- Task: `alty ticket-health` command — implement `TicketHealthPort`
- Task: Two-tier ticket generation (near-term full AC, far-term stubs, promotion)

## Dependencies

- Phases 1–6 complete (all done)
- DDD.md defines Ticket Freshness and Knowledge Base bounded contexts (done)
- ARCHITECTURE.md defines `.alty/` directory structure (done)
- PRD §5.1 specifies `.alty/` layout (done)
- Ripple review spike (k7m.12) provides complete design (done: `docs/research/20260223_ripple_review_design.md`)
- Existing ports: `DocHealthPort`, `KnowledgeLookupPort`, `TicketHealthPort` (done, stub implementations)
- Existing domain models: `TicketHealthReport`, `DocHealthReport`, `ticket_freshness.py` (done, stub implementations)

## Risks / Unknowns

- **RLM addressing scheme** — How to make knowledge base entries addressable by topic? Needs spike.
- **doc-registry format** — TOML structure, fields, review cadence calculation. Needs spike.
- **Ripple review domain boundary** — `bin/bd-ripple` shells out to `bd` CLI. Moving into domain means an ACL around beads (DDD.md says ACL). Risk is lower than expected — existing script is well-structured and already enforces invariants.
- **Knowledge content scope** — How much seed content ships with alty? Start minimal, expand based on usage.
- **Two-tier depth calculation** — Spike defines ≤2 hops = near-term, >2 = far-term. Need to verify beads can expose dependency depth efficiently.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alty init` creates `.alty/` directory with `config.toml`, `knowledge/`, and `maintenance/doc-registry.toml`
- [ ] `alty init --existing` detects missing `.alty/` as a gap in gap analysis
- [ ] Generated CLAUDE.md includes after-close protocol (spike §6.2–6.3 format)
- [ ] `alty kb <topic>` returns content from `.alty/knowledge/` (not "not yet implemented")
- [ ] `alty doc-health` checks freshness, broken references, missing metadata
- [ ] `alty doc-review` marks docs as reviewed with `last_reviewed` timestamps
- [ ] `alty ticket-health` reports `review_needed` tickets with context diffs and per-epic freshness percentage
- [ ] Ripple review lifecycle operates through domain services with tests (not bash-only)
- [ ] Two-tier generation: near-term tickets get full AC, far-term get stubs; Core subdomain override
- [ ] All child tasks closed with quality gates passed
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` (linting)
- [ ] `uv run mypy .` (type checking)
- [ ] `uv run pytest --coverage --min-coverage=80` (tests with 80% coverage)
- [ ] QA was done for each child task before close

title: Spike: .alty/ directory structure and RLM addressing scheme
description:
## Research Question

- What is the minimal `.alty/` directory structure that satisfies PRD §5.1?
- How should RLM (Resource Locator Model) addressing work for `alty kb <topic>` lookups?
- What is the right format for `.alty/maintenance/doc-registry.toml`?
- How do we seed `.alty/knowledge/` — copy from a bundled template, or generate from domain model?

## Timebox

**Timebox:** 3 hours

## Background / Context

- PRD §5.1 defines the `.alty/` directory layout: `config.toml`, `knowledge/` (DDD, tools, conventions), `maintenance/doc-registry.toml`
- ARCHITECTURE.md §7.6 specifies metadata fields: `last_verified`, `verified_against`, `confidence`, `next_review_date`
- `KnowledgeLookupPort` already exists (`src/application/ports/knowledge_lookup_port.py`) with `lookup(category, topic, version)` signature
- No implementation exists yet — need to decide file organization, addressing convention, and content format

## Investigation Approach

1. **Review PRD §5.1 and ARCHITECTURE.md §7.6** — extract all required structure, fields, and behaviors
2. **Survey RLM patterns** — how do similar tools (Context7, MkDocs, Sphinx) address knowledge by topic?
3. **Prototype `.alty/` layout** — draft the directory tree, TOML schemas, and a few sample knowledge files
4. **Validate against existing port** — confirm proposed structure maps cleanly to `KnowledgeLookupPort.lookup(category, topic, version)` signature
5. **Define seed strategy** — bundled template files vs generated content vs hybrid

## Expected Deliverables

- [ ] Research report at `docs/research/YYYYMMDD_alty_directory_structure.md`
- [ ] Proposed `.alty/` directory tree with example files
- [ ] `config.toml` schema definition
- [ ] `doc-registry.toml` schema definition
- [ ] RLM addressing convention (how `alty kb ddd/aggregates` maps to files)
- [ ] Seed content strategy recommendation
- [ ] Follow-up tickets created (if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Research question(s) answered
- [ ] Findings documented
- [ ] Proposed structure validated against `KnowledgeLookupPort` signature
- [ ] Recommendation provided
- [ ] Follow-up tickets created (if implementation needed)
- [ ] Timebox respected (or extended with justification)
close_reason: Research report complete: docs/research/20260304_alty_directory_structure.md. Key finding: RLM addressing, doc-registry, knowledge reader all already implemented. Remaining work is seed content authoring and CLI wiring.

title: alty ticket-health command — implement TicketHealthPort
description:
## Goal / Problem

`alty ticket-health` currently prints "not yet implemented". Implement the command to report ticket freshness: flagged tickets with context diffs, per-epic freshness percentage, and oldest unreviewed ticket. Uses the existing `TicketHealthHandler` and `BeadsTicketReader`.

## Background / Context

- `TicketHealthHandler` already defined in `src/application/queries/ticket_health_handler.py` with `TicketReaderProtocol`
- `TicketHealthReport` domain model exists in `src/domain/models/ticket_freshness.py` with `freshness_pct` property (delivered by 2j7.9)
- `EpicHealthSummary` VO exists in `src/domain/models/ticket_freshness.py` (delivered by 2j7.9)
- `BeadsTicketReader` ACL adapter exists at `src/infrastructure/external/beads_ticket_reader.py`
- CLI stub exists at `src/infrastructure/cli/main.py`
- Ripple review spike defines full command behavior: flagged list, freshness %, thresholds
- Thresholds: 90-100% healthy, 70-89% acceptable, <70% action needed
- **Critical gap:** JSONL export lacks labels and interactions. Adapter must shell out to `bd` CLI.
- **Pattern reference:** Follow `alty kb` / `alty doc-health` CLI wiring pattern

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Ticket Freshness (Core) |
| Ubiquitous Language | Ticket health, freshness percentage, review_needed, context diff, stale |
| Layer | Application (query handler) + Infrastructure (beads ACL adapter) |

## Design

### Data Models (all exist)

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `TicketHealthReport` | VO (exists) | Aggregated health metrics with `freshness_pct` |
| `FlaggedTicket` | VO (exists) | One flagged ticket with trigger info and context |
| `EpicHealthSummary` | VO (exists) | Per-epic freshness: total, fresh, stale counts |
| `BeadsTicketReader` | ACL adapter (exists, needs refactor) | Implements `TicketReaderProtocol` via `bd` CLI |

### Adapter Refactor (Key Change)

Current `BeadsTicketReader` reads JSONL files directly, but:
- JSONL has no labels (labels live in Dolt DB only)
- JSONL has no interactions/comments (interactions.jsonl is empty)

**Fix:** Shell out to `bd` CLI instead:
- `bd query label=review_needed` for flagged ticket list
- `bd show <id>` for ticket details
- `bd comments <id>` for ripple comments
- This is the ACL pattern: bd CLI is the stable interface

### Ripple Comment Regex Fix

Current: `**Ripple context diff from \`<id>\`:**`
Actual bd-ripple output: `**Ripple review needed** -- \`<id>\`...` + `**What changed:** ...`

### Output Format

```
Ticket Health Report
====================

Overall freshness: 85% (acceptable)
  Open tickets: 20
  Flagged (review_needed): 3

Flagged tickets:
  ! alty-abc -- triggered by alty-xyz (2 days ago)
    Context: "Added new StackProfile for Go"
  ! alty-def -- triggered by alty-xyz (2 days ago)
    Context: "Added new StackProfile for Go"

Oldest unreviewed: alty-ghi (5 days)
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S** | Adapter reads beads data; handler builds report; CLI displays |
| **O** | New health checks added as methods, not modifying existing |
| **L** | `BeadsTicketReader` honors `TicketReaderProtocol` contract |
| **I** | `TicketReaderProtocol` has focused methods (read_open_tickets, read_flags) |
| **D** | CLI depends on handler, handler depends on protocol, not adapter directly |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
tests/infrastructure/external/test_beads_ticket_reader.py (extend existing)
test_read_open_tickets_via_bd_cli: mocks subprocess, returns parsed tickets with labels
test_read_flags_via_bd_comments: mocks subprocess, parses ripple comments
test_bd_not_found_returns_empty: missing bd binary returns empty tuple

tests/infrastructure/cli/test_ticket_health_cli.py (new)
test_ticket_health_shows_freshness_pct: displays percentage with threshold label
test_ticket_health_shows_flagged_list: lists flagged tickets with context
test_ticket_health_no_flagged_shows_healthy: 100% healthy when clean
test_ticket_health_exit_code_1_on_issues: exit 1 when flagged tickets exist
test_ticket_health_epic_flag: --epic filters to specific epic
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/infrastructure/cli/test_ticket_health_cli.py` -> should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Refactor `BeadsTicketReader` to shell out to `bd` CLI for labels + comments
2. Fix `_RIPPLE_PATTERN` regex to match actual comment format
3. Wire CLI `ticket-health` stub to `TicketHealthHandler`
4. Format `TicketHealthReport` as terminal output
5. Add `--epic <id>` flag using `EpicHealthSummary`

Run: `uv run pytest` -> should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify comment parsing handles malformed comments gracefully
- Ensure subprocess calls have timeouts
- Verify error messages to stderr

## Steps

1. Refactor `BeadsTicketReader` to use `bd` CLI subprocess calls
2. Fix ripple comment regex to match actual format
3. Wire CLI `ticket-health` command to handler (replace stub)
4. Format terminal output with freshness %, flagged list, threshold labels
5. Add `--epic <id>` flag for epic-scoped view
6. Write/extend tests for adapter, CLI, and handler

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alty ticket-health` shows overall freshness percentage with threshold label
- [ ] Lists flagged tickets with triggering ticket ID and context diff
- [ ] Shows oldest unreviewed ticket
- [ ] Thresholds: 90-100% healthy, 70-89% acceptable, <70% action needed
- [ ] `--epic <id>` shows per-epic freshness using `EpicHealthSummary`
- [ ] `BeadsTicketReader` reads labels via `bd query` (not JSONL)
- [ ] `BeadsTicketReader` reads comments via `bd comments` (not JSONL)
- [ ] Ripple comment regex matches actual bd-ripple output format
- [ ] Errors to stderr, exit 1 when flagged tickets exist
- [ ] All tests pass, coverage >= 80%

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No open tickets | All closed | 100% healthy, nothing to report |
| No flagged | All clean | 100% healthy |
| All flagged | Every open ticket has review_needed | 0% -- action needed |
| Malformed comments | Comment without ripple format | Skip, don't crash |
| No beads | Project without `.beads/` | Error: "No beads tracking found" |
| bd not installed | `bd` binary not on PATH | Error: "beads CLI (bd) not found" |
| Subprocess timeout | bd hangs | Timeout after 10s, report error |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest
```

title: Two-tier ticket generation (near-term full AC, far-term stubs, promotion)
description:
## Goal / Problem

The ticket pipeline currently generates all tickets at the same detail level. Implement two-tier generation: near-term tickets get full acceptance criteria, far-term tickets get stubs. Stubs are promoted to full detail when their blockers resolve (via ripple review).

## Background / Context

- PRD §5 P0: "Two-tier ticket generation: near-term tickets get full AC, far-term tickets are stubs until promoted"
- Ripple review spike §5 defines the algorithm:
  - **Depth-based:** ≤2 hops from root = near-term (full), >2 hops = far-term (stub)
  - **Subdomain override:** Core always gets full detail regardless of depth
  - **Promotion:** When ripple review clears blockers, stub becomes eligible for promotion
- `TicketDetailRenderer` in `src/domain/services/ticket_detail_renderer.py` already has `FULL`, `STANDARD`, `STUB` levels
- `TicketDetailLevel` enum in `src/domain/models/ticket_values.py`
- Ticket pipeline in `src/application/commands/ticket_gen_handler.py`
- Beads stub template: `docs/beads_templates/beads-stub-template.md`
- **Pattern reference:** Follow existing `TicketDetailRenderer` pattern (detail level → sections)

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Ticket Pipeline (Core) + Ticket Freshness (Core) |
| Ubiquitous Language | Two-tier generation, near-term, far-term, stub, promotion, depth |
| Layer | Domain (rendering rules) + Application (pipeline handler) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `TicketDepth` | Value Object | Hop count from epic root to this ticket |
| `TierClassification` | Value Object | near_term or far_term with reason |
| Stub template | Markdown | Minimal ticket body for far-term work |

### Classification Algorithm

```
def classify_tier(depth: int, subdomain: SubdomainClassification) -> TierClassification:
    if subdomain == Core:
        return near_term  # Core always full, regardless of depth
    if depth <= 2:
        return near_term  # Close to root = actionable soon
    return far_term  # Deep in graph = defer detail
```

### Promotion Flow

```
Ripple review fires (ticket closes)
    → Flagged tickets checked
    → If flagged ticket is a stub AND all blockers resolved:
        → Mark as "promotion_eligible"
        → Agent grooms stub into full ticket during pick-up
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S** | Classification logic separate from rendering; promotion separate from flagging |
| **O** | New classification rules (e.g., team size) added without modifying existing |
| **L** | `TierClassification` works with existing `TicketDetailLevel` enum |
| **I** | Classification is a pure function, no interface needed |
| **D** | Pipeline depends on classification function, not concrete rules |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
tests/domain/models/test_tier_classification.py
test_core_always_near_term: Core subdomain at depth 5 → near_term
test_depth_2_near_term: Supporting at depth 2 → near_term
test_depth_3_far_term: Supporting at depth 3 → far_term
test_generic_depth_1_near_term: Generic at depth 1 → near_term
test_generic_depth_3_far_term: Generic at depth 3 → far_term

tests/domain/services/test_ticket_detail_renderer_tiers.py
test_near_term_renders_full: near_term ticket gets FULL detail
test_far_term_renders_stub: far_term ticket gets STUB detail
test_pipeline_assigns_tiers: ticket pipeline uses depth to classify

tests/domain/models/test_stub_promotion.py
test_stub_eligible_when_unblocked: stub with no blockers is promotion_eligible
test_stub_not_eligible_when_blocked: stub with open blockers stays stub
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/models/test_tier_classification.py tests/domain/services/test_ticket_detail_renderer_tiers.py` → should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `TierClassification` VO with `classify_tier()` function
2. Wire classification into ticket pipeline (assign depth during generation)
3. Map `near_term` → `FULL`/`STANDARD` and `far_term` → `STUB` detail levels
4. Add promotion eligibility check to ripple review flow
5. Use `beads-stub-template.md` for far-term ticket bodies

Run: `uv run pytest` → should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify depth calculation handles DAG (not just tree) correctly
- Ensure stub template matches `docs/beads_templates/beads-stub-template.md`

## Steps

1. Create `TierClassification` VO with classify function
2. Add depth calculation to ticket pipeline
3. Wire tier classification into `TicketDetailRenderer` call
4. Add promotion eligibility check
5. Verify stub template used for far-term tickets
6. Write tests for classification, rendering, and promotion

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Near-term tickets (depth ≤2) get FULL or STANDARD detail
- [ ] Far-term tickets (depth >2) get STUB detail
- [ ] Core subdomain override: always full detail regardless of depth
- [ ] Stub tickets use `beads-stub-template.md` format
- [ ] Promotion eligibility flagged when blockers resolve
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Coverage >= 80%

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Depth 0 | Epic root | near_term (FULL) |
| Depth 2 | Boundary | near_term |
| Depth 3 | Just past boundary | far_term (STUB) |
| Core at depth 10 | Deep Core ticket | near_term (override) |
| Circular deps | A→B→A | Detect cycle, use max depth seen |
| No parent | Orphan ticket | Depth 0, near_term |

## Quality Gates

- `uv run ruff check .` -- zero errors
- `uv run mypy .` -- zero errors
- `uv run pytest` -- all pass

comments.author: Jaigouk Kim

title: alty doc-health: broken markdown link detection
description:
## Goal / Problem

`alty doc-health` should detect broken internal markdown links. When a document references `[text](path)` and the target path does not exist, this should be reported as a BROKEN_LINK issue.

## Background / Context

- Split from 2j7.7 (doc-health CLI wiring). The CLI wiring is done, but link checking was deferred.
- Original AC specified: 'alty doc-health reports broken markdown links'
- Edge cases from 2j7.7: external URLs skipped, anchor links skipped, no infinite loops on circular refs
- Existing infrastructure: `FilesystemDocScanner` at `src/infrastructure/persistence/filesystem_doc_scanner.py`
- Existing domain: `DocHealthStatus` enum at `src/domain/models/doc_health.py` (needs BROKEN_LINK variant)
- **Pattern reference:** Follow existing `FilesystemDocScanner._scan_registered()` pattern (iterate entries, check conditions, build DocStatus)

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Knowledge Base (Supporting) |
| Ubiquitous Language | Broken link, internal reference, doc health |
| Layer | Domain (new status) + Infrastructure (link extraction + validation) |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `DocHealthStatus.BROKEN_LINK` | Enum variant (new) | Status for docs with broken internal links |
| `BrokenLink` | Value Object (new) | Source file, line number, link text, target path, reason |

### Sequence / Flow

```
FilesystemDocScanner     doc file     target path
    |                      |              |
    |-- read content ----> |              |
    |<-- markdown ---------|              |
    |-- extract links -----|              |
    |-- resolve path ------+------------> |
    |                      |    exists?   |
    |<-- yes/no -----------+------------- |
    |-- build DocStatus ---|              |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S** | Link extraction separate from validation; scanner orchestrates both |
| **O** | New link types (e.g., image refs) added without modifying existing checks |
| **L** | BROKEN_LINK status works with existing DocHealthReport contract |
| **I** | Scanner interface unchanged -- broken links reported via existing DocStatus |
| **D** | Scanner depends on filesystem abstraction, not direct I/O |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
tests/infrastructure/persistence/test_broken_link_detection.py
test_detects_broken_internal_link: [text](nonexistent.md) reported as BROKEN_LINK
test_skips_external_urls: [text](https://example.com) not checked
test_skips_anchor_links: [text](#section) not checked
test_resolves_relative_paths: [text](../other/doc.md) resolved from doc dir
test_multiple_broken_links_all_reported: 3 broken links in one doc all listed
test_no_broken_links_ok: doc with all valid links stays OK/STALE (unchanged)
test_circular_refs_no_hang: A links to B, B links to A -- no infinite loop
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/infrastructure/persistence/test_broken_link_detection.py` -> should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Add `BROKEN_LINK` variant to `DocHealthStatus` enum
2. Add `BrokenLink` VO to domain models (file, line, text, target, reason)
3. Implement `_extract_markdown_links(content)` helper in scanner
4. Implement `_check_broken_links(links, doc_path, project_dir)` in scanner
5. Integrate link checking into `_scan_registered()` flow
6. Update CLI output to display BROKEN_LINK with file and line

Run: `uv run pytest` -> should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify regex handles edge cases (nested brackets, escaped chars)
- Ensure link extraction is efficient (single-pass regex)

## Steps

1. Add `BROKEN_LINK` to `DocHealthStatus` enum in domain layer
2. Create `BrokenLink` VO in `src/domain/models/doc_health.py`
3. Implement link extraction regex in `FilesystemDocScanner`
4. Implement link validation (resolve relative paths, check existence)
5. Integrate into `_scan_registered()` and `_scan_unregistered()`
6. Update CLI output formatting for BROKEN_LINK status
7. Write tests for all layers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Broken internal markdown links detected and reported as BROKEN_LINK
- [ ] External URLs (https://, http://) skipped
- [ ] Anchor-only links (#section) skipped
- [ ] Relative paths resolved correctly
- [ ] No infinite loops on circular references
- [ ] CLI output shows BROKEN_LINK status with file and line number
- [ ] All tests pass, coverage >= 80%

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| External links | `[text](https://...)` | Skip, no issue |
| Anchor links | `[text](#section)` | Skip, no issue |
| Circular refs | A links to B, B links to A | Detect once per broken link, no hang |
| Relative paths | `[text](../other/doc.md)` | Resolve relative to doc's directory |
| Multiple broken links | Same doc, 3 broken links | Report all 3 |
| Mixed valid/broken | 2 valid + 1 broken | Only report the broken one |
| Empty link target | `[text]()` | Report as broken |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest
```
