title: In-memory Discovery adapter
description:
> **Before Starting:** Always groom the ticket first. Ensure the goal is clear,
> acceptance criteria are testable, and steps are well-defined before assigning work.

## Goal / Problem

The DiscoveryPort defines the interface for managing guided discovery sessions (start, detect_persona, answer_question, skip_question, confirm_playback, complete, get_session). composition.py has a _StubDiscovery that raises NotImplementedError.

Implement an InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter that wraps SessionStore + DiscoverySession aggregate, delegating all state transitions to the domain model while managing session persistence in the SessionStore.

## Background / Context

- Port definition: \`src/application/ports/discovery_port.py\` — 7 methods
- Domain model: \`src/domain/models/discovery_session.py\` — DiscoverySession aggregate with full state machine
- Session store: \`src/infrastructure/session/session_store.py\` — TTL-based in-memory store
- Snapshot support: \`to_snapshot()\` / \`from_snapshot()\` on DiscoverySession (added in alty-rhm)
- PRD capabilities supported: C8 (guided project bootstrap), C9 (DDD question framework)
- **Pattern reference:** \`src/application/commands/discovery_handler.py\` — existing handler that creates sessions

### Port Signature Mismatch (must fix in this ticket)

\`DiscoveryPort.confirm_playback(session_id, confirmed)\` is missing the \`corrections\` parameter. The aggregate \`DiscoverySession.confirm_playback(confirmed, corrections="")\` accepts optional corrections text when the user rejects playback.

**Action required:** Extend \`DiscoveryPort.confirm_playback\` to include \`corrections: str = ""\` before implementing the adapter, so the full playback correction flow is supported.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Guided Discovery (Core) — per DDD.md §4 "Context: Guided Discovery" |
| Ubiquitous Language | DiscoverySession (Aggregate), Persona (VO), Register (VO), QuestionPhase (VO), Question (Entity), Answer (VO), Playback (VO), DiscoveryCompleted (Domain Event) |
| Layer | Infrastructure |

## Design

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| \`InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter\` | Adapter | Implements DiscoveryPort via SessionStore + DiscoverySession |

### Sequence / Flow

\`\`\`
CLI / MCP           InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter     SessionStore    DiscoverySession
    |                        |                      |                |
    |-- start_session(readme)->                      |                |
    |                        |-- new DiscoverySession(readme) ------>|
    |                        |-- store.put(id, session) ----------->|
    |                        |<- session ----------------------------|
    |<- session --------------|                      |                |
    |                        |                      |                |
    |-- answer_question(id, qid, ans) ->             |                |
    |                        |-- store.get(id) ---->|                |
    |                        |<- session -----------|                |
    |                        |-- session.answer_question(qid, ans) ->|
    |                        |-- store.put(id, session) ----------->|
    |                        |<- session ----------------------------|
    |<- session --------------|                      |                |
\`\`\`

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | Adapter only bridges port to domain + store |
| **O**pen/Closed | New adapters (Redis, file-based) can implement DiscoveryPort |
| **L**iskov Substitution | Satisfies DiscoveryPort Protocol |
| **I**nterface Segregation | DiscoveryPort has focused session operation methods |
| **D**ependency Inversion | CLI depends on DiscoveryPort, not this class |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

\`\`\`python
# tests/infrastructure/session/test_in_memory_discovery_adapter.py

# --- Happy path ---
def test_start_session_returns_created_session():
    """start_session creates a CREATED session with readme content."""

def test_detect_persona_transitions_to_persona_detected():
    """detect_persona moves session to PERSONA_DETECTED."""

def test_answer_question_records_answer():
    """answer_question adds answer to session."""

def test_skip_question_records_skip():
    """skip_question records skipped question."""

def test_confirm_playback_transitions_to_answering():
    """confirm_playback(confirmed=True) moves PLAYBACK_PENDING to ANSWERING."""

def test_confirm_playback_with_corrections():
    """confirm_playback(confirmed=True, corrections='fix X') records corrections."""

def test_complete_transitions_to_completed():
    """complete moves session to COMPLETED with events."""

def test_get_session_returns_stored_session():
    """get_session retrieves previously stored session."""

def test_full_discovery_flow():
    """Full flow: start -> detect -> answer x10 (with playback) -> complete."""

def test_satisfies_discovery_port_protocol():
    """InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter is a runtime instance of DiscoveryPort."""

def test_multiple_concurrent_sessions():
    """Multiple sessions can coexist in the store."""

# --- Error propagation from aggregate ---
def test_get_session_not_found_raises():
    """get_session with unknown ID raises SessionNotFoundError."""

def test_detect_persona_invalid_choice_raises():
    """detect_persona with '5' raises ValueError."""

def test_detect_persona_when_already_detected_raises():
    """detect_persona twice raises InvariantViolationError."""

def test_answer_question_empty_response_raises():
    """answer_question with '' raises ValueError."""

def test_answer_question_unknown_question_raises():
    """answer_question with 'Q99' raises ValueError."""

def test_answer_question_duplicate_raises():
    """answer_question same Q1 twice raises InvariantViolationError."""

def test_answer_during_playback_raises():
    """answer during PLAYBACK_PENDING raises InvariantViolationError."""

def test_skip_question_empty_reason_raises():
    """skip_question with '' reason raises ValueError."""

def test_skip_during_playback_raises():
    """skip during PLAYBACK_PENDING raises InvariantViolationError."""

def test_complete_without_enough_answers_raises():
    """complete with < MVP answers raises InvariantViolationError."""

def test_complete_during_playback_raises():
    """complete from PLAYBACK_PENDING raises InvariantViolationError."""

def test_expired_session_raises():
    """get_session after TTL raises SessionNotFoundError."""
\`\`\`

### GREEN Phase

1. Extend \`DiscoveryPort.confirm_playback\` signature to include \`corrections: str = ""\`
2. Update \`_StubDiscovery.confirm_playback\` in composition.py to match
3. Create \`src/infrastructure/session/in_memory_discovery_adapter.py\`
4. Initialize with a SessionStore instance
5. Each method: get/create session, delegate to aggregate, put back in store, return session

### REFACTOR Phase

- Ensure SessionNotFoundError propagation is clean
- Verify domain errors (InvariantViolationError, ValueError) propagate unchanged
- Verify TTL behavior with session store

## Steps

1. Extend DiscoveryPort.confirm_playback to add \`corrections: str = ""\` parameter.
2. Update _StubDiscovery.confirm_playback in composition.py to match new signature.
3. Write failing tests for InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter — happy path (10 tests), error propagation (12 tests).
4. Implement InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter in \`src/infrastructure/session/in_memory_discovery_adapter.py\`.
5. Run quality gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DiscoveryPort.confirm_playback updated with \`corrections: str = ""\` parameter
- [ ] InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter implements DiscoveryPort Protocol
- [ ] All 7 port methods delegate correctly to DiscoverySession aggregate
- [ ] Sessions persist in SessionStore between calls
- [ ] confirm_playback passes corrections through to aggregate
- [ ] Domain errors (InvariantViolationError, ValueError) propagate unchanged to callers
- [ ] SessionNotFoundError raised for unknown/expired session IDs
- [ ] Multiple concurrent sessions supported
- [ ] Full discovery flow works: start → detect → answer x10 (with playback) → complete
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Unknown session ID | get_session("bogus") | SessionNotFoundError |
| Expired session | get after TTL | SessionNotFoundError |
| Invalid persona choice | detect_persona(id, "5") | ValueError (from aggregate) |
| Persona already detected | detect_persona twice | InvariantViolationError |
| Empty answer | answer_question(id, "Q1", "") | ValueError |
| Unknown question | answer_question(id, "Q99", "x") | ValueError |
| Duplicate answer | answer_question(id, "Q1", ...) twice | InvariantViolationError |
| Answer during playback | answer while PLAYBACK_PENDING | InvariantViolationError |
| Skip during playback | skip while PLAYBACK_PENDING | InvariantViolationError |
| Skip empty reason | skip_question(id, "Q1", "") | ValueError |
| Playback with corrections | confirm_playback(id, True, "fix actors") | Corrections recorded in Playback VO |
| Complete without MVP | complete() with < 5 MVP answers | InvariantViolationError |
| Complete during playback | complete() while PLAYBACK_PENDING | InvariantViolationError |
| Multiple sessions | 2 concurrent sessions | Both operate independently |

## Quality Gates

\`\`\`bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
\`\`\`

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- No formal dependencies — uses existing SessionStore and DiscoverySession.
- SessionStore TTL (30 min default) should be sufficient for interactive sessions.
- Port signature change is backwards-compatible (\`corrections\` has default value).

title: Wire composition root with real adapters
description:
> **Before Starting:** Always groom the ticket first. Ensure the goal is clear,
> acceptance criteria are testable, and steps are well-defined before assigning work.

## Goal / Problem

`src/infrastructure/composition.py` currently returns _Stub* classes for most ports. Phase 2 adapters need to be wired into the `create_app()` factory, replacing stubs with real implementations. Additionally, `file_writer` and `artifact_renderer` need to be added to AppContext so CLI commands can construct handlers via proper DI.

## Background / Context

- Composition root: `src/infrastructure/composition.py` — `create_app()` returns AppContext with **11** ports
- Phase 2 real adapters to wire:
  - **Replace stubs on AppContext:**
    - `discovery` → `InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter(store=SessionStore())` (cww.3, done)
    - `quality_gate` → `QualityGateHandler(runner=SubprocessGateRunner())` — QualityGateHandler already satisfies `QualityGatePort` Protocol
  - **Add new ports to AppContext:**
    - `file_writer: FileWriterPort` → `FilesystemFileWriter()` (cww.1, done)
    - `artifact_renderer: ArtifactRendererPort` → `MarkdownArtifactRenderer()` (cww.2, done)
- Remaining stubs (Phase 3+): BootstrapPort, TicketGenerationPort, ConfigGenerationPort, FitnessGenerationPort, DocHealthPort, DocReviewPort, TicketHealthPort, SpikeFollowUpPort
- **Pattern reference:** Current `create_app()` function, `detect` command handler construction

### Why add file_writer and artifact_renderer to AppContext?

All 4 generate commands (artifacts, fitness, tickets, configs) need FileWriterPort. ArtifactRendererPort is needed by ArtifactGenerationHandler. Adding them to AppContext avoids construction duplication across CLI commands and enables consistent DI.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Bootstrap (Generic infrastructure wiring) |
| Ubiquitous Language | AppContext, composition root, port, adapter |
| Layer | Infrastructure |

## Design

### AppContext Changes

```python
@dataclass
class AppContext:
    # Existing ports (replace stubs):
    discovery: DiscoveryPort          # → InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter
    quality_gate: QualityGatePort     # → QualityGateHandler(SubprocessGateRunner())
    # New ports (add to AppContext):
    file_writer: FileWriterPort       # → FilesystemFileWriter()
    artifact_renderer: ArtifactRendererPort  # → MarkdownArtifactRenderer()
    # Remaining stubs unchanged:
    bootstrap: BootstrapPort
    tool_detection: ToolDetectionPort
    fitness_generation: FitnessGenerationPort
    ticket_generation: TicketGenerationPort
    config_generation: ConfigGenerationPort
    doc_health: DocHealthPort
    doc_review: DocReviewPort
    ticket_health: TicketHealthPort
    spike_follow_up: SpikeFollowUpPort
```

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI main.py         create_app()         Real Adapters
    |                    |                    |
    |-- create_app() --->|                    |
    |                    |-- InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter(SessionStore()) -->|
    |                    |-- QualityGateHandler(SubprocessGateRunner()) -->|
    |                    |-- FilesystemFileWriter() -->|
    |                    |-- MarkdownArtifactRenderer() -->|
    |                    |<-- AppContext --------------|
    |<-- ctx ------------|                    |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | create_app() only wires dependencies |
| **O**pen/Closed | New adapters slot in without changing handlers |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Handlers receive Protocol interfaces, not concrete classes |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```python
# tests/infrastructure/test_composition.py (update existing tests)
def test_create_app_returns_app_context():
    """create_app returns an AppContext instance."""

def test_discovery_is_real_adapter():
    """AppContext.discovery is InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter, not stub."""

def test_quality_gate_is_real_handler():
    """AppContext.quality_gate is QualityGateHandler, not stub."""

def test_file_writer_is_real_adapter():
    """AppContext.file_writer is FilesystemFileWriter."""

def test_artifact_renderer_is_real_adapter():
    """AppContext.artifact_renderer is MarkdownArtifactRenderer."""

def test_remaining_stubs_raise_not_implemented():
    """Ports not yet wired still raise NotImplementedError."""
```

### GREEN Phase

1. Add `file_writer: FileWriterPort` and `artifact_renderer: ArtifactRendererPort` to AppContext dataclass
2. Add TYPE_CHECKING imports for FileWriterPort, ArtifactRendererPort
3. Update `create_app()` to instantiate real adapters for discovery, quality_gate, file_writer, artifact_renderer
4. Remove replaced stub classes (_StubDiscovery, _StubQualityGate)
5. Keep stubs for ports not yet wired

### REFACTOR Phase

- Remove dead stub code for replaced ports
- Ensure clean import structure

## Steps

1. Write failing tests verifying real adapters are wired and new ports exist.
2. Add `file_writer` and `artifact_renderer` fields to AppContext dataclass.
3. Update `create_app()` to use real adapters: InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter, QualityGateHandler(SubprocessGateRunner()), FilesystemFileWriter(), MarkdownArtifactRenderer().
4. Remove replaced stub classes.
5. Update test_app_context_has_all_ports to include new fields.
6. Run quality gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AppContext has `file_writer: FileWriterPort` and `artifact_renderer: ArtifactRendererPort` fields
- [ ] `create_app()` returns AppContext with InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter for discovery
- [ ] `create_app()` returns AppContext with QualityGateHandler(SubprocessGateRunner()) for quality_gate
- [ ] `create_app()` returns AppContext with FilesystemFileWriter() for file_writer
- [ ] `create_app()` returns AppContext with MarkdownArtifactRenderer() for artifact_renderer
- [ ] _StubDiscovery and _StubQualityGate removed
- [ ] Remaining stubs still present for Phase 3+ ports
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Default construction | create_app() with no args | All real adapters instantiated |
| Protocol compliance | isinstance checks | All real adapters satisfy their Protocol |
| Stub ports | bootstrap.preview() | Still raises NotImplementedError |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-cww.2 (MarkdownArtifactRenderer) — cww.1 and cww.3 already done
- SessionStore used by InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter has 30-min TTL — sufficient for CLI sessions

> **IMPORTANT:** Dependencies listed here are documentation only. You MUST also set
> formal dependencies with `bd dep add <this-ticket> <depends-on>` so that
> `bd blocked` / `bd ready` / ripple review can see them.

title: CLI alty guide — interactive discovery session
description:
> **Before Starting:** Always groom the ticket first. Ensure the goal is clear,
> acceptance criteria are testable, and steps are well-defined before assigning work.

## Goal / Problem

`alty guide` currently prints a stub message. It needs to run the full interactive 10-question DDD discovery flow: persona detection, dual-register question presentation, answer collection, playback confirmation loops, and session completion.

Wire the CLI `guide` command to the `DiscoveryPort` from AppContext (backed by InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter after cww.4), providing an interactive Typer-based Q&A session.

## Background / Context

- CLI stub: `src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:guide()` — currently constructs a standalone DiscoveryHandler and prints "session started"
- **DiscoveryHandler is now redundant:** `InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter` (cww.3, done) does the same thing (wraps DiscoverySession aggregate) but uses SessionStore with TTL instead of a bare dict. After cww.4 wires `AppContext.discovery` → `InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter`, the CLI should use `AppContext.discovery` directly, not construct a separate DiscoveryHandler.
- Domain model: `src/domain/models/discovery_session.py` — DiscoverySession with CREATED → PERSONA_DETECTED → ANSWERING ↔ PLAYBACK_PENDING → COMPLETED state machine
- Question catalog: `src/domain/models/question.py` — Question.CATALOG with 10 questions, dual register (technical/non_technical), 5 phases
- Persona detection: choice "1"-"4" maps to DEVELOPER/PRODUCT_OWNER/DOMAIN_EXPERT/MIXED
- Playback: every 3 answers triggers PLAYBACK_PENDING, user confirms or provides corrections
- **Pattern reference:** `src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:detect()` — constructs handler directly, but after cww.4 guide should use `create_app().discovery`

### Wiring Pattern

```python
from src.infrastructure.composition import create_app

ctx = create_app()
# ctx.discovery is InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter — use directly
session = ctx.discovery.start_session(readme_content)
```

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Guided Discovery (Core) |
| Ubiquitous Language | DiscoverySession, Persona, Register, Question, Answer, Playback, QuestionPhase |
| Layer | Infrastructure (CLI) |

## Design

### Sequence / Flow

```
User              CLI guide()         AppContext.discovery    DiscoverySession
  |                   |                     |                      |
  |-- alty guide ---->|                     |                      |
  |                   |-- create_app() ---->|                      |
  |                   |-- start_session --->|-- new session ------>|
  |<-- "Who are you?" |                     |                      |
  |-- "1" (developer)->                     |                      |
  |                   |-- detect_persona -->|-- detect_persona -->|
  |<-- Q1 prompt -----|                     |                      |
  |-- answer -------->|-- answer_question ->|-- answer_question ->|
  |   ... repeat x10, with playback every 3 answers ...           |
  |<-- "Session complete!" --|              |                      |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | CLI guide() only handles I/O; domain logic in DiscoverySession |
| **O**pen/Closed | MCP server uses same DiscoveryPort without CLI changes |
| **L**iskov Substitution | InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter satisfies DiscoveryPort |
| **I**nterface Segregation | CLI depends on DiscoveryPort, not full AppContext |
| **D**ependency Inversion | CLI gets adapter via composition root |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```python
# tests/infrastructure/cli/test_cli_guide.py
def test_guide_command_starts_session(cli_runner):
```

### GREEN Phase

1. Update `src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:guide()` to call `create_app()` and use `ctx.discovery`
2. Implement interactive loop: persona → questions → playback → complete
3. Use `typer.prompt()` for input, `typer.echo()` for output
4. Remove standalone `DiscoveryHandler()` construction

### REFACTOR Phase

- Extract CLI I/O helpers for consistent formatting
- Ensure register-appropriate question text is displayed

## Steps

1. Write failing CLI integration tests using Typer's CliRunner with input simulation.
2. Update `guide()` to call `create_app()` and use `ctx.discovery` (InMemoryDiscoveryAdapter).
3. Implement interactive loop: persona detection → question presentation (register-aware) → answer/skip → playback confirmation → completion.
4. Handle corrections during playback rejection (pass `corrections` param).
5. Show session summary on completion.
6. Run quality gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `alty guide` uses `AppContext.discovery` (not standalone DiscoveryHandler)
- [ ] Prompts for persona detection (1-4 choice)
- [ ] Presents questions using detected persona's register (technical vs non-technical)
- [ ] Collects answers via interactive prompt
- [ ] Supports skip with reason
- [ ] Triggers playback confirmation every 3 answers
- [ ] Supports playback corrections (corrections param passed to confirm_playback)
- [ ] Completes session and shows summary
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Invalid persona choice | "5" or "abc" | Re-prompt with valid options |
| Empty answer | "" (enter) | Re-prompt or reject |
| Skip all questions | "skip" for all | Session needs MVP answers, warn user |
| Ctrl+C during session | Keyboard interrupt | Graceful exit with message |
| Playback rejection | User says "no" to playback | Prompt for corrections text |
| Playback with corrections | "fix actors" | Corrections recorded, continue answering |

## Quality Gates

```bash

uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-cww.4 (composition wiring provides AppContext with real discovery adapter)
- Interactive CLI testing with CliRunner can be tricky — may need input simulation via `input` parameter.
- SessionStore TTL (30 min) should be sufficient for interactive sessions.

title: CLI alty generate — subcommand wiring
description:
> **Before Starting:** Always groom the ticket first. Ensure the goal is clear,
> acceptance criteria are testable, and steps are well-defined before assigning work.

## Goal / Problem

The \`alty generate\` subcommands (artifacts, fitness, tickets, configs) are stubs that print "not yet implemented". Wire each subcommand to its handler via the composition root, implementing the preview-before-action pattern.

Each subcommand should: load a completed DiscoverySession → derive DomainModel → call the handler's build_preview() → display preview → prompt for approval → call approve_and_write().

## Background / Context

- CLI stubs: \`src/infrastructure/cli/generate.py\` — 4 subcommands, all stubs
- Handlers (all implemented and tested):
  - \`src/application/commands/artifact_generation_handler.py\` — ArtifactGenerationHandler.build_preview(event: DiscoveryCompleted)
  - \`src/application/commands/fitness_generation_handler.py\` — FitnessGenerationHandler.build_preview(model: DomainModel, root_package: str)
  - \`src/application/commands/ticket_generation_handler.py\` — TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview(model: DomainModel)
  - \`src/application/commands/config_generation_handler.py\` — ConfigGenerationHandler.build_preview(model: DomainModel, tools: tuple[...])
- Preview-before-action pattern: build_preview() → user reviews → approve_and_write()
- All handlers need FileWriterPort (from AppContext after cww.4)
- **Pattern reference:** Preview pattern in ARCHITECTURE.md Design Principle 3

### DomainModel Loading Design

Standalone \`alty generate\` commands (outside \`alty init\`) need a DomainModel. The design:

1. **\`alty guide\` saves session snapshot** to \`.alty/session.json\` on completion (via DiscoverySession.to_snapshot())
2. **\`alty generate\` loads snapshot** via DiscoverySession.from_snapshot() → complete() → get DiscoveryCompleted event
3. **ArtifactGenerationHandler** derives DomainModel from DiscoveryCompleted event
4. **Other handlers** receive that DomainModel directly

Handler input types:
- \`ArtifactGenerationHandler.build_preview(event: DiscoveryCompleted)\` — takes event, derives model internally
- \`FitnessGenerationHandler.build_preview(model: DomainModel, root_package: str)\` — takes model
- \`TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview(model: DomainModel)\` — takes model
- \`ConfigGenerationHandler.build_preview(model: DomainModel, tools: tuple)\` — takes model

So \`alty generate artifacts\` loads session and passes the event, while the other 3 subcommands need to first run the artifact handler's model derivation to get a DomainModel, then pass it to their handler.

**Within \`alty init\` (cww.8):** All in-process. Guide completes → DomainModel derived → passed to all handlers. No snapshot loading needed.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Domain Model (artifacts) + Architecture Testing (fitness) + Ticket Pipeline (tickets) + Tool Translation (configs) |
| Ubiquitous Language | DomainModel, ArtifactPreview, FitnessPreview, TicketPreview, ConfigPreview, DiscoveryCompleted, approve_and_write |
| Layer | Infrastructure (CLI) |

## Design

### Sequence / Flow (standalone generate)

\`\`\`
User              CLI generate X       SessionSnapshot        Handler
  |                   |                     |                     |
  |-- alty generate X->                     |                     |
  |                   |-- load .alty/session.json ->              |
  |                   |<- DiscoverySession --|                     |
  |                   |-- derive DomainModel |                     |
  |                   |-- build_preview -----+-------------------->|
  |                   |<- preview -----------------------------------
  |<-- show preview --|                     |                     |
  |-- "approve" ----->|                     |                     |
  |                   |-- approve_and_write ---------------------->|
  |<-- "Done!" -------|                     |                     |
\`\`\`

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | Each subcommand delegates to its handler |
| **O**pen/Closed | New generate subcommands can be added without modifying existing |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Subcommands get handlers via composition root |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

\`\`\`python
# tests/infrastructure/cli/test_cli_generate.py

# -- Session loading --
def test_generate_artifacts_loads_session_snapshot(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate artifacts loads .alty/session.json."""

def test_generate_artifacts_errors_without_session(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate artifacts fails gracefully with no saved session."""

# -- Preview and write --
def test_generate_artifacts_shows_preview(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate artifacts shows preview before writing."""

def test_generate_artifacts_writes_files_on_approve(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate artifacts writes PRD.md, DDD.md, ARCHITECTURE.md."""

def test_generate_fitness_shows_preview(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate fitness shows fitness test preview."""

def test_generate_fitness_writes_files_on_approve(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate fitness writes importlinter.toml and test_fitness.py."""

def test_generate_tickets_shows_preview(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate tickets shows ticket plan preview."""

def test_generate_tickets_writes_files_on_approve(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate tickets writes ticket markdown files."""

def test_generate_configs_shows_preview(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate configs shows config preview for detected tools."""

def test_generate_configs_writes_files_on_approve(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate configs writes tool-specific config files."""

def test_generate_artifacts_rejects_on_deny(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty generate artifacts does not write if user denies."""
\`\`\`

### GREEN Phase

1. Implement session snapshot loading helper: load \`.alty/session.json\` → from_snapshot() → derive DomainModel
2. Update each subcommand in \`src/infrastructure/cli/generate.py\`
3. Each: load model → construct handler with AppContext ports → build_preview → display → prompt → approve_and_write

### REFACTOR Phase

- Extract common preview-approve-write pattern into helper
- Extract session loading into shared utility
- Ensure consistent CLI output formatting

## Steps

1. Implement session snapshot loading utility (load \`.alty/session.json\`, from_snapshot, derive DomainModel).
2. Write failing tests for all 4 generate subcommands — session loading, preview display, file writing, approval denial.
3. Implement \`artifacts\` subcommand — wire to ArtifactGenerationHandler.
4. Implement \`fitness\` subcommand — wire to FitnessGenerationHandler.
5. Implement \`tickets\` subcommand — wire to TicketGenerationHandler.
6. Implement \`configs\` subcommand — wire to ConfigGenerationHandler.
7. Run quality gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 4 subcommands load DomainModel from saved session snapshot (\`.alty/session.json\`)
- [ ] Graceful error when no saved session exists ("Run alty guide first")
- [ ] \`alty generate artifacts\` builds preview and writes PRD.md, DDD.md, ARCHITECTURE.md
- [ ] \`alty generate fitness\` builds preview and writes importlinter.toml + test_fitness.py
- [ ] \`alty generate tickets\` builds preview and writes ticket markdown files
- [ ] \`alty generate configs\` builds preview and writes tool-specific config files
- [ ] All follow preview-before-action: show preview → prompt → write only on approval
- [ ] Denial does not write any files
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No saved session | No \`.alty/session.json\` | Error: "Run alty guide first" |
| Corrupt snapshot | Invalid JSON in session.json | Error: "Session snapshot is corrupt" |
| Incomplete session | Session not completed | Error: "Discovery session not completed" |
| User denies | "n" at approval prompt | No files written |
| Files already exist | Output files present | Overwrite with warning |

## Quality Gates

\`\`\`bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
\`\`\`

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-cww.4 (composition wiring for FileWriterPort, ArtifactRendererPort)
- Session snapshot format must match from_snapshot() expectations (alty-rhm delivered this)
- The DomainModel derivation path: snapshot → DiscoverySession → complete() → DiscoveryCompleted → ArtifactGenerationHandler._build_model()

> **IMPORTANT:** Dependencies listed here are documentation only. You MUST also set
> formal dependencies with \`bd dep add <this-ticket> <depends-on>\` so that
> \`bd blocked\` / \`bd ready\` / ripple review can see them.

title: CLI alty check — quality gate execution
description:
> **Before Starting:** Always groom the ticket first. Ensure the goal is clear,
> acceptance criteria are testable, and steps are well-defined before assigning work.

## Goal / Problem

\`alty check\` currently prints "not yet implemented". Wire it to the QualityGateHandler + SubprocessGateRunner to run all 4 quality gates (lint, types, tests, fitness) and display a formatted report.

## Background / Context

- CLI stub: \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:check()\` — prints stub message
- Handler: \`src/application/commands/quality_gate_handler.py\` — QualityGateHandler.check()
- Runner: \`src/infrastructure/external/subprocess_gate_runner.py\` — SubprocessGateRunner (already implemented)
- Domain model: \`src/domain/models/quality_gate.py\` — QualityGate enum, GateResult, QualityReport
- **Pattern reference:** \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:detect()\` — existing wired CLI command

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Architecture Testing (Supporting) |
| Ubiquitous Language | QualityGate, GateResult, QualityReport, lint, types, tests, fitness |
| Layer | Infrastructure (CLI) |

## Design

### Sequence / Flow

\`\`\`
User              CLI check()        QualityGateHandler     SubprocessGateRunner
  |                   |                   |                       |
  |-- alty check ---->|                   |                       |
  |                   |-- check() ------->|                       |
  |                   |                   |-- run(LINT) --------->|
  |                   |                   |<- GateResult ---------|
  |                   |                   |-- run(TYPES) -------->|
  |                   |                   |<- GateResult ---------|
  |                   |                   |-- run(TESTS) -------->|
  |                   |                   |<- GateResult ---------|
  |                   |                   |-- run(FITNESS) ------>|
  |                   |                   |<- GateResult ---------|
  |                   |<- QualityReport --|                       |
  |<-- formatted report |                 |                       |
\`\`\`

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | CLI formats output; handler runs gates; runner executes commands |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Handler depends on GateRunnerProtocol, not SubprocessGateRunner |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

\`\`\`python
# tests/infrastructure/cli/test_cli_check.py
def test_check_runs_all_gates(cli_runner):
    """alty check runs lint, types, tests, fitness gates."""

def test_check_displays_pass_results(cli_runner):
    """Passing gates show green checkmarks."""

def test_check_displays_fail_results(cli_runner):
    """Failing gates show red X marks."""

def test_check_returns_nonzero_on_failure(cli_runner):
    """Exit code 1 when any gate fails."""

def test_check_returns_zero_on_all_pass(cli_runner):
    """Exit code 0 when all gates pass."""

def test_check_shows_duration(cli_runner):
    """Each gate result includes execution duration."""

def test_check_specific_gate(cli_runner):
    """alty check --gate lint runs only lint."""
\`\`\`

### GREEN Phase

1. Update \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:check()\`
2. Construct QualityGateHandler with SubprocessGateRunner
3. Call handler.check(), format QualityReport for display
4. Set exit code based on report.all_passed

### REFACTOR Phase

- Add optional \`--gate\` flag for running specific gates
- Format output with color if terminal supports it

## Steps

1. Write failing tests for CLI check command — all gates, pass/fail display, exit codes, duration.
2. Implement check() in \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py\`.
3. Wire QualityGateHandler to SubprocessGateRunner.
4. Format output as table with pass/fail, duration, and summary.
5. Run quality gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] \`alty check\` runs all 4 quality gates (lint, types, tests, fitness)
- [ ] Displays formatted report with pass/fail status per gate
- [ ] Shows execution duration per gate
- [ ] Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
- [ ] Optional \`--gate\` flag for specific gate selection
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| All pass | Clean project | Exit 0, all green |
| One fails | Lint error | Exit 1, lint red, others green |
| Tool missing | ruff not installed | Graceful "command not found" error |
| No tests/architecture/ | Fitness gate | Skipped gracefully (existing behavior) |
| Invalid gate | --gate bogus | Error: invalid gate name |

## Quality Gates

\`\`\`bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
\`\`\`

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-cww.4 (composition wiring)
- SubprocessGateRunner already exists and is tested — this is purely CLI wiring.

> formal dependencies with \`bd dep add <this-ticket> <depends-on>\` so that

title: CLI alty init — full bootstrap orchestrator
description:
> **Before Starting:** Always groom the ticket first. Ensure the goal is clear,
> acceptance criteria are testable, and steps are well-defined before assigning work.

## Goal / Problem

\`alty init\` (without \`--existing\`) currently prints "not yet implemented". This is the capstone ticket: wire the full bootstrap pipeline that takes a user from README idea to a fully-seeded project.

The flow: detect tools → guided discovery → generate artifacts → generate fitness tests → generate tickets → generate configs → write all to disk. Each step uses the preview-before-action pattern.

## Background / Context

- CLI stub: \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:init()\` — else branch prints "not yet implemented"
- Bootstrap handler: \`src/application/commands/bootstrap_handler.py\` — preview/confirm/execute flow
- Bootstrap session: \`src/domain/models/bootstrap_session.py\` — PREVIEWING → CONFIRMED → EXECUTING → COMPLETED
- All generation handlers already implemented and tested (artifacts, fitness, tickets, configs)
- Detection handler: \`src/application/commands/detection_handler.py\` — DetectionHandler (already wired for --existing)
- **Pattern reference:** \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:init(existing=True)\` — rescue flow already wired

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Bootstrap (Core) — orchestrates all other contexts |
| Ubiquitous Language | BootstrapSession, Preview, FileAction, DiscoverySession, DomainModel |
| Layer | Infrastructure (CLI) — orchestration of application handlers |

## Design

### Sequence / Flow

\`\`\`
User         CLI init()     BootstrapHandler    DetectionHandler    guide()    generate()
  |              |                |                   |              |            |
  |-- alty init ->               |                   |              |            |
  |              |-- preview --->|                   |              |            |
  |              |               |-- detect -------->|              |            |
  |              |<- preview ----|                   |              |            |
  |<- "Will create:" --|        |                   |              |            |
  |-- "yes" ---------->|        |                   |              |            |
  |              |-- confirm --->|                   |              |            |
  |              |               |                   |              |            |
  |              |-- guide() (interactive discovery) ------------->|            |
  |              |<- DiscoverySession (completed) -----------------|            |
  |              |               |                   |              |            |
  |              |-- generate artifacts, fitness, tickets, configs ------------>|
  |              |<- preview --------------------------------------------------|
  |<- "Preview:" -----|         |                   |              |            |
  |-- "approve" ----->|         |                   |              |            |
  |              |-- write all ------------------------------------------------>|
  |<- "Done!" --------|         |                   |              |            |
\`\`\`

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | init() orchestrates; each handler does its job |
| **O**pen/Closed | New generation steps can be added without modifying existing handlers |
| **D**ependency Inversion | All handlers receive ports via composition root |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

\`\`\`python
# tests/infrastructure/cli/test_cli_init.py
def test_init_requires_readme(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init fails if no README.md exists."""

def test_init_shows_preview(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init shows preview of planned files."""

def test_init_runs_guided_discovery(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init runs interactive discovery session."""

def test_init_generates_artifacts(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init generates PRD.md, DDD.md, ARCHITECTURE.md."""

def test_init_generates_fitness_tests(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init generates importlinter.toml and test_fitness.py."""

def test_init_generates_tickets(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init generates ticket markdown files."""

def test_init_generates_configs(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init generates tool configs for detected tools."""

def test_init_creates_alty_directory(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """alty init creates .alty/ directory structure."""

def test_init_cancel_writes_nothing(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """Canceling at preview writes no files."""

def test_init_idempotent_skips_existing(cli_runner, tmp_path):
    """Running init twice skips already-existing files."""
\`\`\`

### GREEN Phase

1. Update \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:init()\` else branch
2. Orchestrate: preview → confirm → guide → generate → approve → write
3. Wire all handlers via composition root

### REFACTOR Phase

- Extract step-by-step progress reporting
- Ensure clear error messages at each stage

## Steps

1. Write failing integration tests for the full init flow.
2. Implement orchestration in \`src/infrastructure/cli/main.py:init()\`:
   a. Show preview of planned actions (BootstrapHandler.preview)
   b. Prompt for confirmation
   c. Run guided discovery (reuse guide() logic or call discovery adapter)
   d. Build DomainModel from completed session
   e. Generate artifacts, fitness, tickets, configs with preview-approve pattern
   f. Write all approved outputs to disk
3. Handle errors at each stage with user-friendly messages.
4. Run quality gates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] \`alty init\` runs the full bootstrap pipeline end-to-end
- [ ] Requires README.md in project directory
- [ ] Shows preview of planned file creations
- [ ] Runs interactive guided discovery (or accepts pre-completed session)
- [ ] Generates all artifacts: PRD.md, DDD.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, fitness tests, tickets, tool configs
- [ ] Creates \`.alty/\` directory structure with knowledge base
- [ ] Follows preview-before-action at each generation step
- [ ] Cancel at any stage writes no files
- [ ] Skips files that already exist (idempotent)
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No README.md | Empty directory | "Create a README.md with your project idea first" |
| Cancel at preview | "n" at confirmation | No files written |
| Cancel during discovery | Ctrl+C mid-session | Graceful exit, no partial files |
| Already bootstrapped | \`.alty/\` exists | Skip existing files, warn user |
| Minimal answers | Only MVP questions answered | Generate with available data |
| No tools detected | Fresh system | Generate without tool configs |

## Quality Gates

\`\`\`bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
\`\`\`

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on: alty-cww.4 (composition wiring), alty-cww.5 (CLI guide), alty-cww.6 (CLI generate), alty-cww.7 (CLI check)
- This is the capstone ticket — all other Phase 2 tickets must be complete first.
- Interactive testing complexity: full pipeline involves multiple prompts.

> **IMPORTANT:** Dependencies listed here are documentation only. You MUST also set
> formal dependencies with \`bd dep add <this-ticket> <depends-on>\` so that
> \`bd blocked\` / \`bd ready\` / ripple review can see them.

title: Improve fuzzy title matching in spike follow-up audit
description:
## Goal / Problem

The spike follow-up audit (alty-bcv) uses fuzzy title matching to compare follow-up intents from spike reports against created beads tickets. The current 3-tier strategy (case-insensitive exact, substring-in-ticket, substring-in-intent) misses many legitimate matches because spike reports use different title conventions than beads tickets.

**Observed gap:** Running the audit against real spike reports produced 7 orphans, but manual review showed only 2 are genuine — the other 5 were false negatives caused by title format differences:
- Report: "Task: Implement fitness function generation (import-linter + pytestarch)"
- Ticket: "Implement alty generate fitness (import-linter + pytestarch)"

The "Task:" prefix, "alty" insertion, and rephrasing ("generation" vs "generate") defeat simple substring matching.

## Background / Context

- alty-bcv (in_progress): Created the audit tool with basic fuzzy matching
- SpikeFollowUpAdapter._fuzzy_match in src/infrastructure/persistence/spike_follow_up_adapter.py
- Current strategy: exact (case-insensitive), intent-in-ticket substring, ticket-in-intent substring
- 5 of 8 real follow-up intents are matched by humans but not by the algorithm
- **Pattern reference:** spike_follow_up_adapter.py:100-129 (current _fuzzy_match method)

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Ticket Freshness (improves existing audit accuracy) |
| Ubiquitous Language | FuzzyMatch, FollowUpIntent, FollowUpAuditResult (existing terms) |
| Layer | Infrastructure (SpikeFollowUpAdapter._fuzzy_match improvement) |

## Design

### Proposed Improvements

1. **Prefix stripping** — Remove common prefixes before comparison: "Task:", "Spike:", "Bug:", "(Optional)", "Implement", "alty"
2. **Keyword overlap scoring** — Tokenize both titles into words, compute Jaccard similarity (intersection/union). Match if score exceeds threshold (e.g., 0.5).
3. **Parenthetical extraction** — Extract content in parentheses as secondary match tokens: "(import-linter + pytestarch)" should match tickets mentioning those tools.

### Data Models

No new models needed — this improves the existing _fuzzy_match method.

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | _fuzzy_match still only does matching; new strategies are additive |
| **O**pen/Closed | New matching strategies added to the chain without removing existing ones |
| **L**iskov Substitution | N/A (private method improvement) |
| **I**nterface Segregation | Port contract unchanged — only internal accuracy improves |
| **D**ependency Inversion | No new dependencies introduced |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```python
# tests/infrastructure/persistence/test_spike_follow_up_adapter.py (extend existing)
def test_fuzzy_match_strips_task_prefix():
    """'Task: Implement X' matches 'Implement X'"""
def test_fuzzy_match_strips_alty_prefix():
    """'Implement X' matches 'Implement alty X'"""
def test_fuzzy_match_keyword_overlap():
    """'fitness function generation (import-linter)' matches 'generate fitness (import-linter)'"""
def test_fuzzy_match_parenthetical_content():
    """Parenthetical tokens match: '(import-linter + pytestarch)'"""
def test_fuzzy_match_no_false_positives():
    """Unrelated titles with shared common words do not match"""
```

### GREEN Phase

1. Add prefix stripping to _fuzzy_match
2. Add keyword overlap scoring as fallback tier
3. Add parenthetical content matching

### REFACTOR Phase

- Extract matching into a dedicated FuzzyTitleMatcher class if complexity warrants
- Tune similarity threshold against real spike reports
- Verify no regressions in existing test suite

## Steps

1. Add tests for prefix-stripped matching — "Task: X" vs "X", "Implement alty X" vs "Implement X".
2. Add tests for keyword overlap — tokenize, compute Jaccard, threshold at 0.5.
3. Add tests for false positive prevention — unrelated titles with coincidental shared words.
4. Implement prefix stripping in _fuzzy_match — strip "Task:", "Spike:", "Bug:", "(Optional)" before comparison.
5. Implement keyword overlap scoring — after prefix strip and substring checks fail, tokenize both titles, compute word overlap ratio.
6. Verify against real spike reports — run audit tool, confirm 6 of 8 match (only 2 genuine orphans remain).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] "Task: Implement X" fuzzy-matches "Implement X"
- [ ] "Implement X" fuzzy-matches "Implement alty X"
- [ ] Keyword overlap with score >= 0.5 counts as match
- [ ] Parenthetical content contributes to matching
- [ ] No false positives on unrelated titles sharing common words like "Implement"
- [ ] Real audit against docs/research/ reports <= 3 orphans (down from 7)
- [ ] Existing 11 adapter tests still pass (no regressions)

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Only prefix differs | "Task: Build parser" vs "Build parser" | Match |
| Keyword reorder | "fitness function generation" vs "generate fitness functions" | Match (keyword overlap) |
| Short titles | "Fix bug" vs "Fix the bug" | Match (substring) |
| Unrelated titles | "Build parser" vs "Deploy server" | No match |
| Empty after strip | "Task:" vs "Implement alty detect" | No match |
| Identical parenthetical | "(import-linter)" in both | Match boost |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## QA Before Close

- [ ] Happy path works — improved matching reduces false negatives
- [ ] Edge cases covered (see Edge Cases section)
- [ ] No false positives introduced
- [ ] No regressions in existing 11 adapter tests
- [ ] Real audit against project spike reports validates improvement

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on alty-bcv (in_progress) — extends the adapter it created
- Risk: Over-tuning threshold may cause false positives (two unrelated tickets matching)
- Risk: Keyword overlap on short titles (2-3 words) may be unreliable
- Mitigation: Use keyword overlap only as last-resort tier after exact and substring checks

title: Implement knowledge base drift detection
description:
## Goal / Problem

The knowledge base (.alty/knowledge/) stores tool conventions, DDD patterns, and cross-tool rules as TOML entries. These entries are versioned (k7m.31 added version history directories), but nothing detects when conventions drift — tool updates change config formats, code diverges from documented architecture, or entries become stale.

PRD capability C23 (P1) requires: "Detect tool convention changes between versions, flag stale architecture docs vs actual code structure, suggest updates." Currently alty can look up knowledge (k7m.23) but cannot detect that the knowledge has become incorrect.

## Background / Context

- PRD C23 (P1): Knowledge base drift detection
- PRD Phase 7: Ticket & Doc Health — includes drift detection
- DDD.md glossary: "Drift Detection — Detecting when tool conventions change between versions or when docs diverge from actual code"
- DDD.md §3: Knowledge bounded context — SUPPORTING subdomain (RLM addressing, version tracking, drift detection)
- k7m.23 (closed): Implements alty kb (RLM lookup) — can read entries but not detect staleness
- k7m.30 (closed): Populated TOML entries for all 4 tools
- k7m.31 (closed): Added version history directories for tool knowledge entries
- Spike report: docs/research/20260222_killer_features.md §13 item 5
- **Pattern reference:** DocHealthAdapter in src/infrastructure/persistence/filesystem_doc_scanner.py (scans files for freshness)

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Knowledge Base (extends existing KB with drift awareness) |
| Ubiquitous Language | DriftReport, DriftSignal, ConventionChange, StaleEntry, KnowledgeFreshness |
| Layer | Domain (DriftSignal VO, DriftReport VO) + Application (DriftDetectionPort) + Infrastructure (filesystem scanner comparing versions + optional code scanning) |

## Design

### Drift Detection Strategies

1. **Version-to-version diff** — Compare current TOML entries against previous version entries. Flag fields that changed (new keys, removed keys, changed values).
2. **Doc-vs-code divergence** — Compare documented conventions (e.g., "ruff check" in TOML) against actual project config (pyproject.toml, .cursor/rules, etc.). Flag mismatches.
3. **Staleness by age** — Flag entries not updated within a configurable threshold (e.g., 90 days since tool major version release).

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| DriftSignal | Value Object (frozen) | Single detected drift: entry path, signal type (version_change/doc_code_mismatch/stale), description, severity |
| DriftReport | Value Object (frozen) | Collection of DriftSignals with summary stats (total, by severity, by type) |
| DriftDetectionPort | Protocol | Abstraction for drift scanning |

### Sequence / Flow

```
CLI/MCP              DriftDetectionPort      KnowledgeBase      ProjectFiles
    |                       |                     |                  |
    |-- detect_drift ------>|                     |                  |
    |                       |-- read_entries ---->|                  |
    |                       |-- read_versions --->|                  |
    |                       |-- scan_project ---->|                  |
    |                       |                     |                  |<-- read config
    |<- DriftReport --------|                     |                  |
```

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | DriftSignal only describes one drift; DriftReport only aggregates signals |
| **O**pen/Closed | New drift strategies added by implementing new scanners, not modifying existing |
| **L**iskov Substitution | Any DriftDetectionPort implementation returns valid DriftReport |
| **I**nterface Segregation | DriftDetectionPort is separate from KnowledgeBasePort (read vs detect) |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Application depends on DriftDetectionPort Protocol, not filesystem scanner directly |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```python
# tests/domain/models/test_drift_signal.py
def test_drift_signal_is_frozen():
def test_drift_signal_validates_signal_type():
def test_drift_report_counts_by_severity():
def test_drift_report_empty_when_no_drift():

# tests/infrastructure/persistence/test_drift_detector.py
def test_detects_version_change_in_tool_entry():
def test_detects_doc_code_mismatch():
def test_no_drift_when_entries_match():
def test_handles_missing_version_history():
def test_handles_missing_project_config():
```

### GREEN Phase

1. Create DriftSignal and DriftReport VOs in domain/models
2. Create DriftDetectionPort protocol in application/ports
3. Create filesystem-based drift detector in infrastructure/persistence
4. Wire into composition root and MCP server

### REFACTOR Phase

- Extract version comparison logic into reusable utility
- Ensure DriftReport formatting is consistent with DocHealthReport
- Verify glossary terms match docs/DDD.md

## Steps

1. Create DriftSignal value object in src/domain/models/ — frozen dataclass with entry_path, signal_type enum (version_change, doc_code_mismatch, stale), description, and severity (info, warning, error).
2. Create DriftReport value object — frozen dataclass aggregating DriftSignals with computed properties (total_count, by_severity, by_type, has_drift).
3. Create DriftDetectionPort protocol in src/application/ports/ — single detect method returning DriftReport.
4. Create KnowledgeDriftDetector adapter in src/infrastructure/persistence/ — reads .alty/knowledge/ TOML entries, compares current vs version history, optionally scans project config files for mismatches.
5. Wire DriftDetectionPort into composition root (AppContext) and MCP server tool.
6. Add alty drift-check CLI command (or extend alty doc-health with --knowledge flag).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DriftSignal and DriftReport are frozen domain VOs with no external dependencies
- [ ] DriftDetectionPort is a separate Protocol from KnowledgeBasePort
- [ ] Detector compares current TOML entries against version history directories
- [ ] Version-to-version changes produce DriftSignals with changed field details
- [ ] Report includes severity classification (info/warning/error)
- [ ] No false positives when entries are current and match project config
- [ ] MCP tool exposes drift detection
- [ ] Glossary terms added to docs/DDD.md if not already present

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| No version history | Tool entry exists but no previous versions | Skip version comparison, report info signal |
| No project config | Knowledge entry references config file that does not exist | Report warning signal (cannot verify) |
| Empty knowledge base | No .alty/knowledge/ directory | Return empty DriftReport |
| Multiple drift signals | Entry has version change + doc mismatch | Report both signals separately |
| New tool version | Version directory exists but no current entry | Report error signal (entry may need update) |
| Identical versions | Current matches latest version history | No drift signal for that entry |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-fail-under=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## QA Before Close

- [ ] Happy path works — drift detected when entries change between versions
- [ ] Edge cases covered (see Edge Cases section)
- [ ] Error handling tested — missing dirs, missing configs
- [ ] No regressions in existing knowledge base functionality (1291 tests)
- [ ] Domain layer has no external dependencies
- [ ] Report output is clear and actionable

## Risks / Dependencies

- Depends on k7m.30 (closed) — TOML entries must exist to detect drift
- Depends on k7m.31 (closed) — version history directories must exist for comparison
- Depends on k7m.23 (closed) — extends existing KB infrastructure
- Risk: Doc-vs-code comparison is project-specific (which config files to check varies by tool)
- Risk: False positives if version history entries were manually edited
- Risk: Scope creep — drift detection could expand to code architecture scanning (keep focused on knowledge entries first)

title: Rename Antigravity to Roo Code across project
description:
## Goal / Problem

The k7m.1 research spike confirmed that "Antigravity" was the original project name, now primarily known as "Roo Code". All references throughout the codebase need updating to use the current name.

## Background / Context

- k7m.1 research report (`docs/research/20260222_knowledge_base_structure.md`) section 1.3 documents the rename
- Roo Code uses `.roo/` directory with `rules-*` mode files (not `.antigravity/rules.md`)
- Global config: `~/.roo/` (not `~/.antigravity/`)
- The rename affects product docs, PRD, DDD, shell script, README, and beads tickets
- **Pattern reference:** Research report preserves historical context and should NOT be modified

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Tool Translation (Supporting) |
| Ubiquitous Language | "Roo Code", "mode files", "rules-*" |
| Layer | Infrastructure (tool config generation) |

## Steps

1. Update `CLAUDE.md:7` — project overview tool list reference from Antigravity to Roo Code
2. Update `README.md:11, 46` — description and comparison table entries
3. Update `bin/alty` — shell script references:
   - `.antigravity/` → `.roo/`
   - `rules.md` → `rules-*` mode files
   - `setup_antigravity()` → `setup_roo_code()`
4. Update `docs/PRD.md` — 7+ references including directory structure, tool matrix, capabilities
5. Update `docs/DDD.md:165` — tool translation subdomain reference
6. Update `docs/reference/project_setup_reference.sh:7` — comment
7. Update beads tickets that reference Antigravity (k7m.16, k7m.21, others in JSONL)
8. Do NOT update research reports (they document historical findings)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Zero occurrences of "Antigravity" in non-research docs (research reports preserve historical context)
- [ ] `bin/alty` uses `.roo/` directory and `rules-*` mode file pattern
- [ ] PRD tool matrix shows "Roo Code" with correct config format
- [ ] DDD.md tool translation shows Roo Code
- [ ] Shell script function renamed and tested
- [ ] All quality gates pass

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Research reports | Files in `docs/research/` | Left unchanged (historical context) |
| Case sensitivity | "antigravity" vs "Antigravity" | Both forms updated in non-research files |
| Already-updated tickets | k7m.16, k7m.21 | Verify correct, no double-update |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Manual verification: `grep -ri antigravity` shows only research report hits

## Risks / Dependencies

- `bin/alty` shell script changes need manual testing (no automated tests yet)
- Roo Code config format may need a spike if `.roo/rules-*` pattern is more complex than `.antigravity/rules.md`
- Some beads tickets already updated in previous session (k7m.16, k7m.21) — verify no double-update

## Commit Message Format

```
docs: rename Antigravity to Roo Code across project
```

title: Fix: quality gate failures in composition + discovery port
description:
## Goal

Fix pre-existing mypy errors and test failure that blocked quality gates.

## Issues Found

1. **AppContext missing spike_follow_up** — `SpikeFollowUpPort` and `SpikeFollowUpAdapter` existed but were never wired into the composition root. The MCP server referenced `app.spike_follow_up` causing mypy error and test failure.

2. **DiscoveryPort missing get_session** — `DiscoveryAdapter` implemented `get_session()` but the `DiscoveryPort` Protocol didn't declare it. The MCP server called `app.discovery.get_session()` causing mypy error.

## Fixes Applied

- Added `spike_follow_up: SpikeFollowUpPort` to `AppContext`, created `_StubSpikeFollowUp`, wired in `create_app()`
- Added `get_session(session_id) -> DiscoverySession` to `DiscoveryPort` Protocol and `_StubDiscovery`

## Quality Gates

- ruff: 0 errors
- mypy: 0 errors (was 2)
- pytest: 1413 passed (was 1 failed, 1412 passed)

title: Implementability validation for ticket pipeline and ripple review
description:
## Goal / Problem

alty's ticket pipeline generates well-structured tickets (all sections filled, DDD-aligned, TDD phases documented), and its ripple review system flags tickets when dependencies close. But **neither validates whether a ticket is actually implementable.**

**The bug we hit:** Ticket 20c.5 passed grooming 5+ times (initial groom, 4 ripple reviews from 20c.2/20c.3/20c.8/20c.4) — every review said "unchanged" or "PASS." Yet the ticket had a MAJOR gap: the Design section said "RLM adapter performs iterative web search" without specifying what library, port, or API does the actual searching. LLMClient has text_completion() and structured_output() — neither does web search. The ticket also had port signature inconsistencies between the SOLID/ISP section and the sequence diagram, and didn't account for existing test breakage from new constructor parameters.

**Root cause:** Our review mechanisms check two things:
1. **Structure** (grooming): "Are all template sections filled in?" — Yes, they were.
2. **Freshness** (ripple): "Did the closing ticket invalidate anything?" — No, nothing was contradicted.

Neither asks: **"Could you actually write code from this spec right now?"** That's the implementability gap. Every dependency reference resolves to "magic happens here" and nobody catches it until implementation starts.

**Why this matters for users:** alty generates tickets for users' projects via `alty generate tickets`. Those tickets will have the same problem — well-formed structure, all sections present, but port signatures that don't match actual interfaces, dependencies on libraries that aren't specified, and constructor changes that break existing tests. The user's AI coding tool picks up the ticket, starts implementing, and hits "wait, what does the actual searching?" — wasting a full implementation cycle.

## Background / Context

- **Incident:** 20c.5 grooming failure documented in ripple comment (2026-03-06)
- **PRD reference:** P0 "Ticket freshness & ripple review" (line 135), P0 "Domain story to ticket pipeline" (line 130)
- **Existing ripple review:** `bin/bd-ripple` — traverses dependency graph, flags open tickets with review_needed + comment. Checklist now includes "Compatibility check" item (just added).
- **Existing grooming checklist:** CLAUDE.md step 8 "Implementation Simulation" (just added).
- **Spike reference:** `docs/research/20260223_ripple_review_design.md` (k7m.12)
- **Markdown parsing precedent:** `src/infrastructure/persistence/markdown_spike_parser.py` — regex-based section extraction from markdown, returns structured VOs.

## DDD Alignment

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Bounded Context | Ticket Freshness |
| Ubiquitous Language | ImplementabilityCheck, DesignTraceResult, InterfaceMismatch, UnresolvedDependency, BreakingChange, TicketSection, TicketStructure |
| Layer | Domain (validation VOs + ImplementabilityValidator service) + Application (integrated into ticket pipeline) + Infrastructure (CodebasePortScanner adapter) |

## Design

### Two Integration Points

**1. Ticket Pipeline (at generation time) — automated**
When `alty generate tickets` creates tickets from DDD artifacts, run an implementability check BEFORE presenting to the user. Plugs into `TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview()` after `plan.generate_plan(model, profile)` and before returning the preview.

**Interface (structured objects):**
```python
class ImplementabilityValidator:
    def validate(self, ticket: GeneratedTicket, plan: TicketPlan) -> DesignTraceResult:
        """Validate a single ticket's implementability within the plan context."""
```

**2. Ripple Review (at close time) — manual checklist for MVP**
The compatibility check in `bin/bd-ripple` remains a manual checklist item for the human/agent reviewer. Automated ripple validation (parsing markdown descriptions, comparing against delivered interfaces) is a follow-up ticket — it requires a Python entry point that `bin/bd-ripple` can shell out to, or migrating ripple review from bash to Python.

**Future interface (markdown parsing):**
```python
class ImplementabilityValidator:
    def validate_description(self, description: str) -> DesignTraceResult:
        """Parse a markdown ticket description and check for unresolved ports."""
```

### Data Models

| Model | Type | Location | Purpose |
|-------|------|----------|---------|
| `DesignTraceResult` | Value Object (frozen) | `src/domain/models/ticket_implementability.py` | Structured validation result with findings list |
| `ImplementabilityFinding` | Value Object (frozen) | same | Single finding with severity (CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR) |
| `InterfaceMismatch` | Value Object (frozen) | same | Port/method signature mismatch between sections |
| `UnresolvedDependency` | Value Object (frozen) | same | Reference to port/library that doesn't exist |
| `TicketSection` | Value Object (frozen) | same | Parsed section of a ticket description (heading + content) |
| `TicketStructure` | Value Object (frozen) | same | Full parsed ticket as dict[heading, TicketSection] |

### Infrastructure

| Adapter | Location | Purpose |
|---------|----------|---------|
| `CodebasePortScanner` | `src/infrastructure/external/codebase_port_scanner.py` | Scans `src/application/ports/` for port definitions and method signatures |

### Validation Rules

| Rule | What it catches |
|------|----------------|
| **Port resolution** | Design references `WebSearchPort` but no such port exists in `src/application/ports/` |
| **Signature match** | ISP says `research(domain_description, contexts)` but diagram says `research(model)` |
| **Dependency concreteness** | "adapter does web search" — which library? which port? |
| **Breaking change detection** | New constructor param → which existing tests need updating? |
| **Internal consistency** | Does the Design section match the SOLID mapping, sequence diagram, and test plan? |

## SOLID Mapping

| Principle | Implementation |
|-----------|----------------|
| **S**ingle Responsibility | ImplementabilityValidator checks implementability; TicketPipeline generates tickets; RippleReview checks freshness |
| **O**pen/Closed | New validation rules added as VO subtypes without changing validator core |
| **L**iskov Substitution | Validator works with any ticket format that follows the beads template |
| **I**nterface Segregation | Pipeline: `validate(ticket: GeneratedTicket, plan: TicketPlan) -> DesignTraceResult`; Future ripple: `validate_description(description: str) -> DesignTraceResult` |
| **D**ependency Inversion | Validator depends on `CodebasePortScanner` protocol, not concrete implementation |

## TDD Workflow

### RED Phase

```
# tests/domain/models/test_ticket_implementability.py
test_design_trace_result_immutable: Frozen VO
test_design_trace_result_has_findings: findings tuple populated
test_design_trace_result_is_valid_when_no_findings: Empty findings → is_valid=True
test_implementability_finding_severity: CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR values
test_interface_mismatch_vo: section_a, section_b, description
test_unresolved_dependency_vo: port_name, location, description
test_ticket_section_vo: heading + content
test_ticket_structure_get_section: Lookup by heading, returns None for missing

# tests/domain/services/test_implementability_validator.py
test_detects_unresolved_port_reference: Design mentions port not in codebase
test_detects_signature_mismatch: ISP section vs sequence diagram disagree
test_detects_unspecified_dependency: "does web search" without specifying how
test_detects_breaking_constructor_change: New param → existing tests need update
test_detects_internal_inconsistency: Design section contradicts SOLID mapping
test_passes_well_formed_ticket: All references resolve, signatures match
test_returns_structured_result: DesignTraceResult with findings list

# tests/infrastructure/external/test_codebase_port_scanner.py
test_finds_ports_in_application_ports_dir: Scans actual project ports
test_extracts_method_signatures: Port methods with param names + types
test_empty_directory_returns_empty: No ports → empty dict
test_malformed_file_skipped: Non-Python files ignored

# tests/application/commands/test_ticket_generation_handler_validation.py
test_build_preview_runs_validation: Validation integrated into preview
test_validation_findings_included_in_preview: DesignTraceResult in preview
test_validation_passes_clean_ticket: No findings for well-formed ticket

# Regression test
test_reproduces_20c5_failure: The 20c.5 web search gap would have been caught
```

Run: `uv run pytest tests/domain/models/test_ticket_implementability.py tests/domain/services/test_implementability_validator.py` → should FAIL

### GREEN Phase

1. Create `src/domain/models/ticket_implementability.py` — DesignTraceResult, ImplementabilityFinding, InterfaceMismatch, UnresolvedDependency, TicketSection, TicketStructure VOs
2. Create `src/domain/services/implementability_validator.py` — validation logic with rule-based checks
3. Create `src/infrastructure/external/codebase_port_scanner.py` — scans ports directory for definitions
4. Integrate into `TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview()` — validate before returning preview
5. Regression test with 20c.5 scenario

Run: `uv run pytest` → should PASS

### REFACTOR Phase

- Verify validator catches the 20c.5 gaps (regression test green)
- Ensure validation results are surfaced clearly in preview output
- Run quality gates

## Steps

1. Add domain VOs in `src/domain/models/ticket_implementability.py`: DesignTraceResult, ImplementabilityFinding, InterfaceMismatch, UnresolvedDependency, TicketSection, TicketStructure
2. Add ImplementabilityValidator domain service in `src/domain/services/implementability_validator.py`
3. Add CodebasePortScanner infrastructure adapter in `src/infrastructure/external/codebase_port_scanner.py`
4. Integrate into `TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview()` — validate before presenting to user
5. Add regression test reproducing the 20c.5 failure
6. Update `docs/DDD.md` glossary with new terms
7. Leave ripple review integration as manual checklist (current state); create follow-up ticket for automated ripple validation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ImplementabilityValidator detects unresolved port/protocol references via CodebasePortScanner
- [ ] Detects method signature mismatches between ticket sections (ISP vs diagram vs test plan)
- [ ] Detects unspecified dependencies ("magic happens here" pattern)
- [ ] Detects breaking constructor changes that affect existing tests
- [ ] Detects internal ticket inconsistencies (Design vs SOLID vs sequence diagram)
- [ ] Integrated into `TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview()` — validation runs before preview
- [ ] DesignTraceResult VO carries structured findings with severity (CRITICAL/MAJOR/MINOR)
- [ ] CodebasePortScanner infrastructure adapter scans `src/application/ports/` for port definitions and method signatures
- [ ] TicketSection and TicketStructure VOs parse markdown ticket descriptions into structured sections
- [ ] Ripple review compatibility check remains manual checklist in MVP (automated follow-up ticket created)
- [ ] Regression test: reproduces the 20c.5 failure (would have caught the web search gap)
- [ ] DDD.md glossary updated with new terms (DesignTraceResult, ImplementabilityFinding, InterfaceMismatch, UnresolvedDependency, TicketSection, TicketStructure)
- [ ] >= 80% test coverage

## Edge Cases

| Case | Input | Expected Output |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| All references resolve | Well-formed ticket | PASS, no findings |
| Multiple unresolved refs | 3 missing ports | All 3 listed as findings |
| Ticket has no Design section | Stub/incomplete ticket | Skip validation, warn |
| Port exists but wrong signature | Partial match | MISMATCH finding with details |
| No codebase context available | New project, no ports dir | Skip codebase checks, only check internal consistency |
| TicketPlan with mixed quality | Some tickets clean, some broken | Per-ticket results, not all-or-nothing |

## Quality Gates

```bash
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
uv run pytest --coverage --min-coverage=80
```

- [ ] Lint passes
- [ ] Type check passes
- [ ] All tests pass with >= 80% coverage
- [ ] Edge cases have test coverage

## Pre-Implementation Validation

- [ ] `TicketGenerationHandler.build_preview()` exists and accepts injection point after `plan.generate_plan()`
- [ ] `GeneratedTicket` and `TicketPlan` VOs are importable from `src/domain/models/ticket_plan.py` / `ticket_values.py`
- [ ] `src/application/ports/` directory has discoverable `.py` files with Protocol classes
- [ ] `MarkdownSpikeReportParser` pattern in `src/infrastructure/persistence/` is reusable for section extraction
- [ ] `InvariantViolationError` available for VO validation

## Risks / Dependencies

- This is a cross-cutting concern touching ticket pipeline and ripple review
- PRD lines 130 and 135 already include implementability validation
- Complexity: Parsing ticket descriptions to extract port references uses regex (following MarkdownSpikeReportParser precedent), not heavyweight NLP
- MVP approach: Automated validation in ticket pipeline only. Ripple review stays manual. Follow-up ticket for automated ripple validation.
- CodebasePortScanner uses Python AST or importlib inspection — infrastructure concern, not domain logic
